issue_title: relateditems .pattern-relateditems-result-browse links broken on non-site rootPath
issue_body:
If you set anything other than the site root as the rootPath (e.g. a navroot/subsite) in options for the relateditems pattern, the path passed to the AJAX catalog query will be incorrect.  This leads to missing results in browse mode everywhere outside of the rootPath.

To duplicate (using mockup master), something like the following:

* Site's nav root is at `/mysite/my-navroot-folder` -- so...
* `rootPath` could be set to `/mysite/my-navroot-folder` in my pattern options
  * _I use a subclass of the server-side widget to inject this, but that seems a trivial detail._
* site root is `/mysite`
* A folder "animals" exists at `/mysite/my-navroot-folder/animals`
* Clicking the .pattern-relateditems-browse-results link for "animals" will lead to erroneous path:
  * `/mysite/my-navroot-folder/my-navroot-folder/animals` is passed to `@@getVocbaulary` catalog query as path, and of course, this path always yields zero results/brains.

There is really no way that a non-site-root rootPath works at present.  I would like to address this, but a fix is a bit elusive, because:

* Some of this hides behind the abstractions of Select2.
* There is no clear way I understand yet in JavaScript to ascertain the site-root-path in a Plone 5 site (without possibly making an AJAX call).
* We cannot always assume that a duplicated path fragment like `my-navroot-folder/my-navroot-folder` is accidental, in the general case.  This is the easiest way to generalize to most situations, but it does not solve all possible navroot path choices, and possibly incurs some unwanted technical debt as a workaround.

@thet any thoughts?

issue_title: Openstack Mitaka dropped auth_plugin support
issue_body:
Starting with Openstack Mitaka support for the auth_plugin functionality was deprecated in python-novaclient (see: https://github.com/openstack/python-novaclient/blob/master/releasenotes/notes/remove-auth-system-b2cd247b8a312b72.yaml) and since the mitaka-eol release it has been removed. #14 saw this coming.

One option is to bring back the pinned dependency to version 3.3.3. This is the last version of python-novaclient with support for auth_plugin (albiet with the depedency warning) and should work fine.

A better solution would be to migrate rackspace_auth_openstack to a keystoneauth plugin, which is supported by python-novaclient. I will open an issue for this upstream and start investigating.

issue_title: bind_to_random_port seemingly does not try a new port when its first try is already bound
issue_body:
bind_to_random seems to fail with errno 13 (Permission denied) on Windows (specifically this is windows 7 enterprise SP1, using python 3.4 with pyzmq 14.4.1) when seemingly the real problem was that the port was already occupied, and it should've tried a new one. As far as I can tell, this could be because the ZMQ library itself is returning the wrong error code, but my repro code (see below) has, on occasion, printed out a port that seemed not to be occupied at time-of-failure. I had also added a print statement into zmq/sugar/socket.py which would print if it ever retired to bind a new port, and I never saw that print happen.

My apologies if this bug really belongs over in the main libzmq project - but I figured might as well start here. Additionally, I would suggest it makes sense to add a "bind_to_available_port" function which simply binds to port 0, has the OS give it an available port, and then returns that to the user. I am doing that now, to overcome my problems with this intermittent bug.

```python
import time
import zmq

while True:
    print(loopnum)
    ctx = zmq.Context()
    sock = ctx.socket(zmq.SUB)
    port = sock.bind_to_random_port("tcp://127.0.0.1")
    print(port)
    sock.close()
    time.sleep(0.1)
    loopnum += 1
```

Thanks!

issue_title: Add NullHandler to logging

issue_title: Parameters aren't validated when passed via constructor
issue_body:
```python
class Task(luigi.Task):
    p = luigi.IntParameter()

print Task(p='hi').p  # 'hi'
```

While the above is a trivial case of "something will probably break down the line," this can introduce subtle bugs when e.g. a date parameter is passed to a datetime parameter (or vice-versa).

My suggestion would be to add some sort of validation, and possibly the ability to tweak the value (similar to parse) to the Parameter interface. The latter would be useful when implementing an e.g. "every N hours" parameter that automatically floors to the nearest hour.

issue_title: Highlight root tasks in UI

It'd be very useful if there was a way to filter the web UI to only show tasks that are root nodes so as to be able to easily see entire task graphs without specifically needing to know the names of those tasks.

issue_title: Don't attempt to filter out json request params with req_obj_type is used.
issue_body:
Consider a simple object like:

```python
class Person(Object):
  description = 'A Person'
  properties = {
    'name': string('The person\'s name.', example='Bob'),
  }
  required = ['name']
```

If we specify a route should use this type as the req_obj_type when defining the route, I don't think it makes sense to filter out additional parameters in the json body that aren't defined in the type. Especially since out of the box additional properties are allowed. If they aren't allowed we should just let the type raise an exception. So currently this json body:

```json
{
  "name": "Bob",
  "age": 22
}
```

Will have the age key removed, even though the type allows additional properties. We should not filter out any parameters when a routes specifies a req_obj_type and instead let the type raise an http error if additional properties are not allowed.

issue_title: Generated code contains Python 3 syntax error

comtypes generated code looking like this:

```python
# values for enumeration '_BoolValue'
True = 1
False = 0
BoolValue_Max = 2147483647
_BoolValue = c_int # enum
IHTMLDOMImplementation._methods_ = [
```

True and False are reserved words in Python 3, so this is a syntax error. It appears that the syntax error when loading the generated code made it repeatedly try to generate the code anew, slowing the application to a crawl. When I edited the cached file, commented out the two lines defining True and False, and re-ran, I stopped seeing errors and the code ran at a normal speed.

Scraps of information available on the web (e.g. [here](http://wutils.com/com-dll/constants/constants-MSHTML.htm)) indicate that `_BoolValue` is an enum provided by MSHTML. issue_title: Metadata dict access or "value unpacking is a joke currently"
issue_body:
Give a section `s` and a property called `"Type"` that has a value (type `str`) of `"calcium imaging"` this is how you currently get to it:
```python
v = s['Type'].values[0].value
print(v)
# 'calcium imaging'
```
I would *suggest* that we add another way, so we have two ways of getting to properties via dict-like-access: 1) `s['key']` and 2) `s.props['key']`. One of them (I think the latter) should give you the actual `nix.Property` while the other should apply magic[1] to directly give you back the "unpacked" value (or list of values), i.e.:

```python
v = s['key']
print(v)
# 1.0 or "str" or [1.0, 2.0, 3.0]

p = s.props['key']
print(p)
Property: {name = Type}
```
This is python after all. What do you guys think?

[1] with magic I mean logic

issue_title: pipenv install updates locked packages I would not have expected
issue_body:
`pipenv install pkg` updates the versions of unrelated packages in Pipfile.lock. This runs counter to an expectation that the new package would be added to Pipfile and Pipfile.lock without touching other stuff.

##### Describe your environment

1. OS Type
1. Python version: `$ python -V`: Python 3.6.3
1. Pipenv version: `$ pipenv --version`: **pipenv**, version 9.0.1

##### Expected result

When I run `pipenv install foo`, everything but `foo` (and any _new_ dependencies it brings in) should be left as-is.

##### Actual result

Running `pipenv install foo` or `pipenv install --ignore-pipfile foo` adds `foo` _and_ upgrades everything else.

##### Steps to replicate

Let's say I'm upgrading an existing project that used requirements.txt:

```shell
$ cat requirements.txt
toposort==1.4
```

Yay pipenv! Let's make the switch:

```shell
$ pipenv --three
Creating a virtualenv for this project…
[...]
Warning: Your Pipfile now contains pinned versions, if your requirements.txt did.
We recommend updating your Pipfile to specify the "*" version, instead.
```

Now we'll install the required package and see what's actually in Pipfile.lock:

```shell
$ pipenv install
Pipfile.lock not found, creating…
[...]

$ jq '.default.toposort.version' < Pipfile.lock
"==1.4"
```

That worked! But there was a warning about pinning the version with Pipfile.lock instead of Pipfile, so let's remove that pin:

```shell
$ sed -i'' -e 's/==1.4/*/' Pipfile
$ grep toposort Pipfile
toposort = "*"
```

Awesome - we're using the lockfile now. Hmm, we really need to install another package, though:

```shell
$ pipenv install yapf
[...]
```

That worked, so now Pipfile.lock has the new package _and_ pre-existing package at its old version, right?

```shell
$ jq '.default.toposort.version' < Pipfile.lock
"==1.5"
```

That... is not what I'd expected. I get the same results in the last couple of steps if I tell pipenv to ignore Pipfile:

```shell
# Repeat all the steps prior to pipenv install yapf, then:
$ pipenv install --ignore-pipfile yapf
$ jq '.default.toposort.version' < Pipfile.lock
"==1.5"
```

issue_title: virtualenv fails to fix_local_scheme
issue_body:
Creating a brand new virtualenv creates a virtualenv where c modules don't work since it can't find pyconfig.h

After tracking it down and comparing it to one that does work, turns out it wasn't creating the local directory correctly in the fix_local_scheme.

Here is the function (with some added print statements):

```
def fix_local_scheme(home_dir, symlink=True):
    """
    Platforms that use the "posix_local" install scheme (like Ubuntu with
    Python 2.7) need to be given an additional "local" location, sigh.
    """
    print "fix_local_scheme: %s" % home_dir
    try:
        import sysconfig
    except ImportError:
        print "ImportError"
        pass
    else:
        if sysconfig._get_default_scheme() == 'posix_local':
            print "posix_local"
            local_path = os.path.join(home_dir, 'local')
            print "local_path: %s exists: %s" % (local_path, os.path.exists(local_path))
            if not os.path.exists(local_path):
                os.mkdir(local_path)
                print "made local_path: %s" % local_path
                for subdir_name in os.listdir(home_dir):
                    if subdir_name == 'local':
                        continue
                    print "subdir_name: %s" % subdir_name
                    copyfile(os.path.abspath(os.path.join(home_dir, subdir_name)), \
                                                            os.path.join(local_path, subdir_name), symlink)

```

os.path.exists(local_path) returns true because the directory has already been created by virtual so then it fails to setup the rest of the local directory.

Turns out it get's created in the `copy_required_modules` section:

```
  Creating parent directories for venv/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/readline-6.2.4.1-py2.7-linux
-x86_64.egg
  Symlinking venv/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/readline-6.2.4.1-py2.7-linux-x86_64.egg/readline.
so
```

issue_title: Adding placeholders to a template after objects already saved breaks things in a bad way.
issue_body:
We are using `fluent-contents` (without `fluent-pages`). We are using the "CMS system" style with dynamic layout switching. A particular layout's template initially had only one placeholder defined in it. Objects using this layout have already been saved to the database and it is working as expected.

We need to add a second placeholder to the existing layout's template. When we do, the new placeholder is detected and rendered in the form, but when we save the form, we get a `ValidationError: '' value must be an integer."` originating in `fluent_contents.admin.genericextensions.BaseInitialGenericInlineFormSet._construct_form`.

While digging through the code, we noticed what looks like a related bug in `BaseInitialGenericInlineFormSet.__get_form_instance()`. The code assumes that the number of forms in the formset equals the number of `Placeholder` objects already saved in the database and that their sequence is a match. We fixed that problem with the following code:

```
    def __get_form_instance(self, i):
        instance = None
        try:
            # Editing existing object. Make sure the ID is passed.
            instance = self.get_queryset().filter(slot=self._initial[i]['slot'])[0]
        except IndexError:
            try:
                # Adding new object, pass initial values
                # TODO: initial should be connected to proper instance ordering.
                # currently this works, because the client handles all details for layout switching.
                queryset_count = self.get_queryset().count()
                values = self.__initial_minus_queryset()[i - queryset_count]

                values[self.ct_field.name] = ContentType.objects.get_for_model(self.instance)
                values[self.ct_fk_field.name] = self.instance.pk
                instance = self.model(**values)
                instance.save()
            except IndexError:
                pass

        return instance
```

This code does two things differently:

1) It gets the `Placeholder` by slot name instead of by index, in case they are out of order (e.g. the template is updated and their positions are reversed).

2) It calls `instance.save()` to persist the new `Placeholder` object to the database as soon as it is needed by the frontend, when the change form page is rendered.

This fixed the `ValidationError`, but on save we got a different error, instead...

```
IntegrityError: duplicate key value violates unique constraint "fluent_contents_placeholde_parent_type_id_451c85966d08dedf_uniq"
DETAIL:  Key (parent_type_id, parent_id, slot)=(10, 100607, main) already exists.
```

It seems that `fluent-contents` is trying to create a `Placeholder` that already exists. Perhaps because we already created it earlier in `__get_form_instance()`?

@sjdines and I spent a couple hours trying to debug this, and it seems pretty complex. Perhaps this would be an easy fix for you, @vdboor, or you could shed some light on it for us.

Should we be trying to create and save a `Placeholder` object as soon as the frontend needs one, or only when content items are saved to a placeholder?

Or should we be trying to fix the `ValidationError` another way?

issue_title: Support RecordBatch
issue_body: Since Kafka 0.11, the `MessageSet` format has had an [updated version](https://cwiki.apache.org/confluence/display/KAFKA/A+Guide+To+The+Kafka+Protocol#AGuideToTheKafkaProtocol-Messagesets) called `RecordBatch`. We should investigate what it would take to support this format, and what the benefits would be. This will probably require significant changes within `protocol.py` to intelligently switch between `MessageSet` implementations based on Kafka broker version.

issue_title: ARCHFLAGS is required on OS X
issue_body:
This is an anticipatory issue that doesn't (yet) affect master, but will when #1579 is merged.

When `python setup.py build` fails, the cause, and path to resolution is not immediately obvious.  For example, the failure includes a serioues of assembly-related errors, ending with:

```
In file included from nupic/bindings/algorithms_wrap.cxx:4368:
In file included from /Users/amarshall/nta/nupic/extensions/core/build/release/include/nupic/math/SparseMatrix.hpp:38:
/Users/amarshall/nta/nupic/extensions/core/build/release/include/nupic/math/ArrayAlgo.hpp:3910:45: error: register %rsp is only available in 64-bit mode
                   "movaps (%%rsp), %%xmm4\n\t"     // a time
                                            ^
<inline asm>:16:12: note: instantiated into assembly here
        addq $16, %rsp
                  ^~~~
fatal error: too many errors emitted, stopping now [-ferror-limit=]
8 warnings and 20 errors generated.
error: command '/usr/bin/clang' failed with exit status 1
```

The solution is to ensure that `ARCHFLAGS="-arch x86_64"` is set in the environment.  Either at invocation: `ARCHFLAGS="-arch x86_64" python setup.py build`, or set in the shell in advance.

As long as this is an issue, there should be some sort of warning message, or an outright forced error before the use gets too far into the process.

1. A Python logging formatter consistent with [mozlog](https://github.com/mozilla-services/Dockerflow/blob/master/docs/mozlog.md)

2. A Django middleware that emits up [request.summary](https://github.com/mozilla-services/Dockerflow/blob/master/docs/mozlog.md#application-request-summary-type-requestsummary) following the Dockerflow specs

issue_title: build fails on Solaris 11
issue_body:
The `dirfd()` workaround for older versions does not work here (as it's `d_fd`, rather than `dd_fd`), and is in fact not needed, as Solaris 11 provides `dirfd()`.
```
    building '_posixsubprocess' extension
    creating build/temp.solaris-2.11-sun4v.64bit-2.7
    gcc -DNDEBUG -g -O3 -Wall -D_XPG6 -fPIC -I/datapool/dima/Sage/sagetrac-mirror/local/include/python2.7 -c _posixsubprocess.c -o build/temp.solaris-2.11-sun4v.64bit-2.7/_posixsubprocess.o
    _posixsubprocess.c: In function ‘_close_open_fds_maybe_unsafe’:
    _posixsubprocess.c:334:50: error: ‘DIR {aka struct <anonymous>}’ has no member named ‘dd_fd’; did you mean ‘d_fd’?
             int fd_used_by_opendir = dirfd(proc_fd_dir);
                                                      ^~~
                                                      d_fd
    error: command 'gcc' failed with exit status 1
```

If I export `CFLAGS` with `CFLAGS="$CFLAGS -DHAVE_DIRFD"` then the build works.

I am not sure what the correct way to fix this is. `HAVE_DIRFD` needs to be set somehow, I guess.

issue_title: Add automated deployment of final and rcs to PyPI
issue_body:
Here are some adapted from the Python Tracker

```
$ cd /vagrant
$ /home/vagrant/snowplow-python-3.3-tracker-environment/bin/python setup.py register
$ /home/vagrant/snowplow-python-3.3-tracker-environment/bin/python setup.py sdist upload
```

If you get an error like this:

```
hard linking CHANGES.txt -> snowplow-tracker-0.4.0
error: snowplow-tracker-0.4.0/CHANGES.txt: Operation not permitted
```

it will be because you are running in Vagrant in a folder which is shared with your host machine. Copy it out to `/tmp` and run again:

```
$ rm -rf /tmp/snowplow-python-tracker
$ cp -r /vagrant /tmp/snowplow-python-tracker
$ cd /tmp/snowplow-python-tracker
$ /home/vagrant/snowplow-python-3.3-tracker-environment/bin/python setup.py sdist upload
```

issue_title: A goodbye hand wave for log out screens!
Site Design Suggestion: Current Website Design
issue_body:
I understand you guys are REALLY excited about the new FA5, I am too, I even pre-purchased it, but do you REALLY need that many banners? AND the email subscription list thing? 
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1113496/27339028-a5a49e3e-55a4-11e7-9d18-f789ba8547ba.png)
Look at how pretty and easy to use the site is when I removed all the space-wasting crap from it! 
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1113496/27338855-3f2dda6c-55a4-11e7-87b1-97e7db64860e.png)
Makes it so much easier to use.
Issues I have with the current site design:

1. I mistake the email subscription box thing, as the icon search box, why is this thing the first box on the icon page? This should be in the footer, not the first thing on the page.
2. Giant banners/headers take up so much space, I can't actually see what I'm looking for.
3. No icon search box on homepage, Why?

What I think a good solution would require:

1. Remove the stupid FA5 banner, OR Closeable, and remember when I close the banner, so it doesn't pop back up again.
2. Remove the stupid email subscription box from the top of the page, put it in the footer, or somewhere else.
3. put an icon search box on the homepage!
4. If you don't want to remove the page banner (The thing that says stuff like "The Icons!") at least make it take up less space... like, why does it have to take up half of the total page space? Also, isn't this why the navbar says the word "icons" do you really need to re-iterate that the current page is for icons? I don't think it's really needed... in the slightest. Just make the navbar slightly larger, to make the buttons easier to click.

Obviously this is just my opinion, what does everyone else think?

(If you are a member of the FA5 repo, you may have already seen this, this is because I followed their advise and moved it from there to here, because it doesn't really pertain to FA5 for the time being.)

Here is the uBlockOrigin filter for getting rid of the banners that I felt were unneeded:

```
! 6/20/2017, 10:33:11 AM http://fontawesome.io/icons/
fontawesome.io##.hidden-print.jumbotron-ad.jumbotron

! 6/20/2017, 10:33:17 AM http://fontawesome.io/icons/
fontawesome.io###social-buttons

! 6/20/2017, 10:33:48 AM http://fontawesome.io/
fontawesome.io##.hidden-print.jumbotron-carousel.jumbotron

! 6/20/2017, 10:34:05 AM http://fontawesome.io/icons/
fontawesome.io###banner

```

issue_title: Font does not show up on Apple Pages for Mac font selection pulldown on the sidebar
issue_body:
After installing the OTF version of FontAwesome on a Mac, it is not possible to select the font using the Format sidebar font selection pulldown in *Pages* because the font does not show up in the list at all.

If text already formatted using the FontAwesome font is insert into a *Pages* document and a cursor is placed on that text, the font pulldown shows an empty font name. Clicking on that pulldown makes the font "jump" to default to the first font sorted alphabetically.

The same problem does not happen on *Word for Mac*. The font shows up properly.

The same problem does not happen if using the OS font dialog box. The font shows up properly.

Although this may be a *Pages* problem, it is possible that some setting in the font is making it not show properly. Fixing this would make the font much more accessible to users using *Pages*.

A screenshot of the empty font pulldown when FontAwesome text is selected is as follows:

![Screenshow of problem](http://i.imgur.com/hOjawzc.png)

issue_title: Improper URL formation on #iefix font file call
issue_body:
On [this line](https://github.com/FortAwesome/Font-Awesome/blob/c6e79ae1a1e7bef4e7290e5026b704d24f4fa590/css/font-awesome.css#L10):

`url('../fonts/fontawesome-webfont.svg?v=4.6.3#fontawesomeregular)` is properly formatted according to [RFC 3986.4.2](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc3986#section-4.2), with the fragment coming after the query string.

`url('../fonts/fontawesome-webfont.eot?#iefix&v=4.6.3')` is improperly formatted, with the query string coming *inside* the fragment, where a lot of servers won't properly parse it.

issue_title: Improve icon search on the official site
issue_body:
Right now the provided search box by Angolia only looks for the query in the name of the icon.
This usually doesn't result in very useful results as it is basically the same as doing a CTRL+F in the site.

Something far more useful would be adding a couple of keyboards to each icon so users can easily find the icon they are looking for.

A good example of this can be found on [glyphsearch](http://glyphsearch.com/?library=font-awesome) where one can easily find the `fa-smile-o` by looking for the term *face*, for example.

Right now glyphsearch doesn't seem to have all fontawesome icons and I guess it won't get updated at the same time as fontawesome does. 
So seeing this in the official website would be a huge bonus to me, and I guess, for many others who sometimes spend minutes looking through all the 600+ icons just to find the one we are looking for.

issue_title: rejoin former master fails
issue_body:
I'm not entirely sure if this is expected behaviour or not. If I stop a master (systemctl stop patroni) a slave takes over. This works fine. If I then want to rejoin the master as slave using just systemctl start patroni it fails: 

> < 2016-03-31 09:13:38.119 CEST >ERROR:  requested starting point 0/26000000 on timeline 5 is not in this server's history
> < 2016-03-31 09:13:38.119 CEST >DETAIL:  This server's history forked from timeline 5 at 0/250001A8.

I can fix it by manually running pg_rewind on the former master:
` sudo -u postgres pg_rewind -D /var/lib/pgsql/9.5/data --source-server='host=currentmaster password=pwforpostgres' 
`
After running that command the former master can join again as slave and everything appears to work. Looking at the code I see that there is quite a bit of code there for running pg_rewind already so I am wondering if I have hit a bug or if this is how it is expected to work and I should always manually intervene before rejoining a former master even if it was shut down manually (not crashed.)

issue_title: File download doesn't work
issue_body:
I'm trying to use connexion to return a requested file, such that the 200 response has a binary string as its body. I've tried two ways so far and neither have worked due to what looks like Connexion issues.

Swagger yaml fragment:
```
  /deployments/{deployment_id}/avatars/{avatar_filename}:
    get:
      tags:
      - "Avatars"
      summary: "Download an avatar"
      operationId: "mypackage.controller.get_avatar"
      produces:
      - "application/octet-stream"
      parameters:
      - $ref: "#/parameters/deployment_id"
      - $ref: "#/parameters/avatar_filename"
      responses:
        200:
          description: Avatar file
          # Choose one of the schema's below
          # Problem 1 occurs if schema has type: file
          schema:
            type: file
          # Problem 2 occurs if schema has type: string
          schema:
            type: string
            format: binary
        201:
          description: "Avatar uploaded"
```

### Problem 1
According to http://swagger.io/specification/#responseObject, the schema of a response object may have type: file. However, this fails with error when validating the spec (before a request is made):
```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "bin/python", line 52, in <module>
    exec(compile(__file__f.read(), __file__, "exec"))
  File "mysourcedir/main.py", line 68, in <module>
    main(debug)
  File "mysourcedir/main.py", line 56, in main
    app.add_api('swagger.yaml', base_path='/0.1', **kwargs)
  File "myeggsdir/eggs/connexion-1.0.84-py2.7.egg/connexion/app.py", line 144, in add_api
    debug=self.debug)
  File "myeggsdir/eggs/connexion-1.0.84-py2.7.egg/connexion/api.py", line 89, in __init__
    validate_spec(compatibility_layer(self.specification))
  File "myeggsdir/eggs/swagger_spec_validator-2.0.2-py2.7.egg/swagger_spec_validator/validator20.py", line 82, in validate_spec
    http_handlers=http_handlers)
  File "myeggsdir/eggs/swagger_spec_validator-2.0.2-py2.7.egg/swagger_spec_validator/common.py", line 22, in wrapper
    sys.exc_info()[2])
  File "myeggsdir/eggs/swagger_spec_validator-2.0.2-py2.7.egg/swagger_spec_validator/common.py", line 17, in wrapper
    return method(*args, **kwargs)
  File "myeggsdir/eggs/swagger_spec_validator-2.0.2-py2.7.egg/swagger_spec_validator/validator20.py", line 125, in validate_json
    cls=Draft4Validator)
  File "myeggsdir/eggs/swagger_spec_validator-2.0.2-py2.7.egg/swagger_spec_validator/ref_validators.py", line 34, in validate
    instance_cls(schema, *args, **kwargs).validate(instance)
  File "myeggsdir/eggs/jsonschema-2.5.1-py2.7.egg/jsonschema/validators.py", line 123, in validate
    raise error
swagger_spec_validator.common.SwaggerValidationError: {'description': 'Avatar file', 'schema': {'type': 'file'}} is not valid under any of the given schemas

Failed validating u'oneOf' in schema[u'properties'][u'paths'][u'patternProperties'][u'^/'][u'properties'][u'get'][u'properties'][u'responses'][u'patternProperties'][u'^([0-9]{3})$|^(default)$']:
    {u'oneOf': [{u'$ref': u'#/definitions/response'},
                {u'$ref': u'#/definitions/jsonReference'}]}

On instance[u'paths']['/deployments/{deployment_id}/avatars/{avatar_filename}'][u'get'][u'responses']['200']:
    {'description': 'Avatar file', 'schema': {'type': 'file'}}
```
Note that the schema segment above (using the first schema, commenting out the second) is valid according to http://editor.swagger.io/.

### Problem 2
To work around problem 1, we instead try to return a binary string (octet-stream). My handler code is:
```
def get_avatar(deployment_id, avatar_filename):
    avatar_path = os.path.join(settings.AVATAR_DIR, avatar_filename)

    with open(avatar_path, 'r') as f:
        return f.read()
```
This string clearly isn't necessarily utf-8 encoded (it's the contents of a binary file), but Connexion assumes it is at https://github.com/zalando/connexion/blob/master/connexion/decorators/response.py#L57, when it tries to encode it as JSON.
Full error:
```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "myeggsdir/eggs/Flask-0.10.1-py2.7.egg/flask/app.py", line 1817, in wsgi_app
    response = self.full_dispatch_request()

    rv = self.handle_user_exception(e)
  File "myeggsdir/eggs/Flask-0.10.1-py2.7.egg/flask/app.py", line 1381, in handle_user_exception
    reraise(exc_type, exc_value, tb)
  File "myeggsdir/eggs/Flask-0.10.1-py2.7.egg/flask/app.py", line 1475, in full_dispatch_request
    rv = self.dispatch_request()
  File "myeggsdir/eggs/Flask-0.10.1-py2.7.egg/flask/app.py", line 1461, in dispatch_request
    return self.view_functions[rule.endpoint](**req.view_args)
  File "myeggsdir/eggs/connexion-1.0.84-py2.7.egg/connexion/decorators/validation.py", line 245, in wrapper
    response = function(*args, **kwargs)
  File "myeggsdir/eggs/connexion-1.0.84-py2.7.egg/connexion/decorators/produces.py", line 85, in wrapper
    data, status_code, headers = self.get_full_response(function(*args, **kwargs))
  File "myeggsdir/eggs/connexion-1.0.84-py2.7.egg/connexion/decorators/response.py", line 82, in wrapper
    self.validate_response(data, status_code, headers)
  File "myeggsdir/eggs/connexion-1.0.84-py2.7.egg/connexion/decorators/response.py", line 57, in validate_response
    data = json.dumps(data)
  File "myeggsdir/eggs/Flask-0.10.1-py2.7.egg/flask/json.py", line 126, in dumps
    rv = _json.dumps(obj, **kwargs)
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/json/__init__.py", line 250, in dumps
    sort_keys=sort_keys, **kw).encode(obj)
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/json/encoder.py", line 201, in encode
    return encode_basestring_ascii(o)
UnicodeDecodeError: 'utf8' codec can't decode byte 0xa0 in position 4050: invalid start byte
```
It seems like the only solution is to base-64 encode my file before sending, which I shouldn't have to do.

issue_title: Visualization Manager fields missing values
issue_body:
### Context

issue_title: `async` is now a keyword in Python 3.7
issue_body:
**Describe the bug**

Starting in Python 3.7, `async` is a keyword that cannot be used in argument names. Background:

https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0492/#deprecation-plans

jira uses `async` as an argument name for at least one method.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:

* Install Python 3.7.0, which has now been released (I installed it using pyenv)
* Install the `jira` package (I installed it into a pipenv-managed virtualenv)
* `import jira`, see stack trace

**Expected behavior**

The jira package should load and work on Python 3.7

**Stack Trace**
```
...
  File "...", line 3, in <module>
    import jira
  File ".../lib/python3.7/site-packages/jira/__init__.py", line 10, in <module>

SyntaxError: invalid syntax

https://cloud.google.com/appengine/docs/python/ndb/properties#structured

In order to write entities with structured properties, I tried to read them first, to figure out the format.
```Python

[<Entity[{'kind': u'Item', 'id': 5710239819104256L}] {u'meta.xy': [100L, 200L]}>]

Now ```LocalStructuredProperty```.

class Item_Meta(ndb.Model):

issue_title: pip install gcloud broken
issue_body:
(using a fresh conda env to demonstrate):

```bash
conda create -n gcloud-test python=3 -y
source activate gcloud-test
pip install gcloud
python -c "print('hello world')" 
```

output:
```
Error processing line 2 of /Users/jlowin/anaconda/envs/gcloud-test/lib/python3.5/site-packages/googleapis_common_protos-1.1.0-py3.5-nspkg.pth:

  Traceback (most recent call last):
    File "/Users/jlowin/anaconda/envs/gcloud-test/lib/python3.5/site.py", line 167, in addpackage
      exec(line)
    File "<string>", line 1, in <module>
  KeyError: 'google'

Remainder of file ignored
hello world
```

After some trial and error, it looks like the current version of protobuf (3.0.0b2.post1) is causing the problem. Based on the solution to #1304, reverting to a prior version resolves it (or at least suppresses the error):
```
pip install protobuf==3.0.0b1.post2
python -c "print('hello world')" 
```

clean up:
```
source deactivate
conda env remove -n gcloud-test -y
```

issue_title: Subclassing tuple causes failure on de-serialization (but not serialization)
issue_body:
Simple code example:

```python
class Range(tuple):
    def __new__(cls, start, end):
        if start < 0 or end < 0:
            raise ValueError
        if start > end:
            raise ValueError("Range start is before end")
        return tuple.__new__(cls, (start, end))


new_range = Range(0,5)
a = dill.dumps(new_range)

#Exception happens here
dill.loads(a)
```


```
In [7]: dill.loads(a)
---------------------------------------------------------------------------
TypeError                                 Traceback (most recent call last)
<ipython-input-7-46e4242c3d83> in <module>()
----> 1 dill.loads(a)

/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/dill/dill.pyc in loads(str)
    271     """unpickle an object from a string"""
    272     file = StringIO(str)
--> 273     return load(file)
    274
    275 # def dumpzs(obj, protocol=None):

/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/dill/dill.pyc in load(file)
    261     pik = Unpickler(file)
    262     pik._main = _main_module
--> 263     obj = pik.load()
    264     if type(obj).__module__ == _main_module.__name__: # point obj class to main
    265         try: obj.__class__ == getattr(pik._main, type(obj).__name__)

/usr/lib/python2.7/pickle.pyc in load(self)
    856             while 1:
    857                 key = read(1)
--> 858                 dispatch[key](self)
    859         except _Stop, stopinst:
    860             return stopinst.value

/usr/lib/python2.7/pickle.pyc in load_newobj(self)
   1081         args = self.stack.pop()
   1082         cls = self.stack[-1]
-> 1083         obj = cls.__new__(cls, *args)
   1084         self.stack[-1] = obj
   1085     dispatch[NEWOBJ] = load_newobj

TypeError: __new__() takes exactly 3 arguments (2 given)
```


I think this is because when subclassing an immutable object like tuple, and using the ```__new__``` function, the `*args` upon serialization get stored as the `tuple` accepts them, as a single tuple object, i.e `args = ((0,5),)` whereas upon deserialization, the class definition of our `Range` class expects it as `start, end`, i.e `args=(0,5,)`

One fix is by changing the definition of `__new__` method to accept `*args` and check `args[0]` for the type (whether or not it is a tuple) and go on to return the object based on parsing arguments, but was wondering if this was a bug or intended behaviour.

issue_title: jvm_binary deploy_excludes changed behavior as of 0.0.52
issue_body:
I am experimenting with using `deploy_excludes` in junit-runner to fix a problem I'm having with running a particular kind of test suite.  I noticed that building a jar file with 0.0.53 and deploy_excludes=[] appears to be broken, or I don't understand how it is supposed to work.

Here's the patch I was working with:

```
diff --git a/src/java/org/pantsbuild/tools/junit/BUILD b/src/java/org/pantsbuild/tools/junit/BUILD
index 3738214..f72d1cf 100644
--- a/src/java/org/pantsbuild/tools/junit/BUILD
+++ b/src/java/org/pantsbuild/tools/junit/BUILD
@@ -31,6 +31,9 @@ jvm_binary(
   dependencies=[
     ':junit',
   ],
+  deploy_excludes=[
+    exclude(name='junit', org='junit'),
+  ],
   description="""
 A replacement for org.junit.runner.JUnitCore.main that adds:
   + support for running individual test methods using [classname]#[methodname]
```

I created jars with 0.0.51 and 0.0.52 respectively and renamed the output

```
$ ls -l junit*
-rw-r--r--  1 zundel  zundel  2701454 Oct 11 07:03 junit-runner-0.0.51.jar
-rw-r--r--  1 zundel  zundel  3068891 Oct 11 07:17 junit-runner-0.0.52.jar
```

The difference is that the version created with 0.0.51 doesn't contain the org.junit classes, but the one created with 0.0.52 still contains them (as if deploy_excludes was not specified.)

```
$ jar -tvf junit-runner-0.0.51.jar | grep "org/junit" | wc -l
       0
$ jar -tvf junit-runner-0.0.52.jar | grep "org/junit" | wc -l
     284
```

issue_title: extension objects not visible to test runner
issue_body:
# symptom
We have a plugin to compile cython files into an .so that exposes some extension objects. We'd like to use those extension objects in other python build targets, and also use them in unit tests.
Exposing the so for other targets works, in that the .so appears in the pex. However unit tests don't work. The test runner can't find the .so.

Perhaps there something we are not doing in the plugin code to expose the .so to py.test? Do we need to add something to the pythonpath?

# environment
pants: 1.8.0rc0
ubuntu 17.04
target python for the build artifacts: 3.6.3


# Repro
In [pantsbug.zip](https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/files/2138427/pantsbug.zip) is a small toy project that demonstrates the issue. To repro:

```bash
mkdir repro
cd repro
cp ~/Downloads/pantsbug.zip .
unzip pantsbug.zip

# clean slate
rm -rf dist lib/*.h lib/*.so lib/*.cpp /tmp/pantscache && ./pants clean-all

# create pex for app that depends on the cython lib
./pants binary apps/gardener:

# run the pex, this works
dist/gardener.pex

# same thing for the lib itself, also works
dist/shrubbery-lib.pex


# verify the .so are in the pex (they have to be, otherwise running the pex above would not work
unzip -l dist/shrubbery-lib.pex | grep shrubberylib
unzip -l dist/gardener.pex | grep shrubberylib


# running tests does not work sadly, because test runner does not find the .so
./pants test libraries/shrubbery-lib:

# same fail here
./pants test apps/gardener:

```

issue_title: Metrics do not account for v2 engine time
issue_body:
Metrics reported by pants itself do not currently account for the time spent in the v2 engine, which includes all BUILD file parsing and file fingerprinting. This results in a difference between the actual runtime of `time ./pants X` and pants' own reported times (since execution in the engine occurs outside of any workunit).

It's possible that we should solve this _only_ in the case of the daemon, and not concern ourselves with the in-process case.

issue_title: Passing an unrecognized option results in a cryptic error when --verify-config is enabled
issue_body:

issue_title: Add accessors to the Message class
issue_body:
Fedmsg has a lot of accessors available for consumers, let's discuss what we want to keep.

Here's what I found in `fedmsg-meta`, and my comments:
* `title`: replaced by `summary`
* `subtitle`: replaced by `__str__`
* `subjective`: it hasn't really been used, let's drop it
* `long_form` (paragraphs of text about a message): it would be useful for email notifications
* `lexer` (pygments lexer that can be applied to the long_form): not sure we want that, it's only implemented for github and scm messages
* `link`: that would be useful I think (maybe renamed as `url`? I don't find "link" very clear)
* `icon` and `secondary_icon`: those are usually the app icon and the agent's avatar. They are used, so I think we should keep them, but rename them to `app_icon` and `agent_avatar` just to be clearer. (see `fedmsg_meta_fedora_infrastructure.fasshim`).
* `usernames` (a set of FAS usernames associated with the message): this would be necessary for FMN
* `packages` (a set of package names associated with a message): this would be necessary for FMN
* `objects`: unclear, we should drop it and leave it to publishers to provide a precise accessor in their API.
* `emails`: we can leave that to publishers and not make it standard
* `avatars` (dict of avatar URLs associated with a message): unused, drop it

issue_title: GAUGE multiprocess_mode single stat
issue_body:
in some cases, when using gauge in multi process, what I really care about is the latest value updated by any of the workers.
for example, let's say I have a webapp running using uwsgi. this app updates some gauge on each request. what I really want to know is the latest value updated. in that case, I don't really need a separate db file for each pid, a single file would be sufficient to update it and read from it (basically mimicking single process but using a db file to store it across processes)

issue_body:
If an attribute has been defined in the [hash](https://github.com/SUNET/SATOSA/blob/master/doc/README.md#hash) dictionary in the configuration and this attribute is not available for a given transaction, this causes SAtoSA to fail with an 'Unknown error'

The stacktrace is as follows:

```
[2017-04-28 07:48:24] [ERROR]: Unknown error
Traceback (most recent call last):

    return spec(context)
  File "/home/debian/vopaas/virtualenv/lib/python3.4/site-packages/satosa/micro_services/consent.py", line 79, in _handle_consent_response

    return super().process(context, internal_response)

issue_title: Past attempts on the mastery log is always 10, regardless of how many might be needed for the mastery criterion
issue_body:
### Observed behavior
We currently always return the last 10 attempts as part of the MasteryLog serializer: 
https://github.com/learningequality/kolibri/blob/develop/kolibri/logger/serializers.py#L51

This means that for an exercise that requires, e.g. 10 out of 14 correct answers, then a learner who had previously engaged with the exercise could end up having to answer more questions than demanded.

### Expected behavior
Should return all relevant past attempts (i.e. up to `n` of the `m` out of `n` mastery criterion).

### User-facing consequences
Could cause a perceived 'loss of progress' through no fault of the student.

issue_title: Learn: Rendering Content Page Before Promise is Resolved
issue_body:
## Summary

`[Vue warn]: Error when rendering component <content-page> at /home/christian/repos/kolibri/kolibri/plugins/learn/assets/src/vue/content-page/index.vue: `
`TypeError: Cannot read property 'title' of undefined`

## How to reproduce
1. View a piece of content in Learn.
2. Navigate back.
3. Click on the same piece of content.
4. Check console.

## Possible Solution
The issue is in `showLearnContent()` within Learn's actions.js. `CORE_SET_PAGE_LOADING` is set to false by the `recommendedPromise` before the `contentPromise` is resolved.  

There are a few possible fixes. 
1. Add the `recommendedPromise` into the `ConditionalPromise.all` (I believe the separation was to speed up load time.)
2. Let `CORE_SET_PAGE_LOADING` be set to false only by the `contentPromise` (Assumes the `contentPromise` will always resolve last.)
3. Have a counter so that only the promise that is resolved last can set the `CORE_SET_PAGE_LOADING` to false. 

Thoughts @indirectlylit?

issue_title: Images missing from certain Perseus exercises translations
issue_body:
* ricecooker version: 0.6.20
* Python version: all
* Operating System: all


### Description

The method [process_image_field](https://github.com/learningequality/ricecooker/blob/master/ricecooker/classes/questions.py#L274) of the PerseusQuestion class assumes that all images present in the `content` will also be catalogued in the sibling `images` attribute, and fails to create image files when this is the case.

assumption:
```
        {
            "content": "md string including imgs like ![](URL-key) and ![](URL-key2)",
            "images": {
                "URL-key":  {"width": 425, "height": 425},
                "URL-key2": {"width": 425, "height": 425}
            }
        }
```

actual:
```
        {
            "content": "md string including imgs like ![](URL-key) and ![](URL-key2)",
            "images": {}
        }
```
(see http://www.khanacademy.org/api/v1/assessment_items/x43bbec76d5f14f88?lang=bg )



### What I Did

  - Two weeks ago during Q/A the BG team noted many images were missing from the Bulgarian-transaltion of the KA channel
  -  Last week I was trying to debug missing images and found this bug
  - This week I'm going to try to fix it by looking for images in `content` in addition to the `images`

issue_title: If your machine runs out of disk space py-docker returns cannot contact docker host

Try this by running docker on a small SSDs or a VM with little disk space.

issue_title: Allow Highway to work with n-d inputs
issue_body:
Hey guys, great work here, thanks a lot!

I've been using your implementation of the Highway network with text data and I got an error while passing a 3d tensor as input, caused by the following lines: https://github.com/allenai/allennlp/blob/master/allennlp/modules/highway.py#L56

Essentially, the assumption is that the input tensor is 2-d. If the input is BxTxD, this assumption causes an issue. While I think this is OK, and the (BxT -> BT) flattening can be left to the user, there is a simple workaround by using `chunk`:

`nonlinear_part, gate = projected_input.chunk(2, dim=-1)`

Which does the same thing as lines 56 & 57, without the input dimensionality assumption. This could be a nice addition.

issue_body:

On this page: http://soft-matter.github.io/trackpy/tutorial/walkthrough.html

print (frames[0])  # the first frame

print 'Before:', t['particle'].nunique()

frames = pims.ImageSequence('./sample_data/bulk_water/*.png', as_grey=True)

and the page itself needs formatting to make it clearer. e.g. this is all on one line:

image : image array (any dimensions) diameter : feature size in px minmass : minimum integrated brightness  but should be:

image : image array (any dimensions) 
diameter : feature size in px 
minmass : minimum integrated brightness

issue_title: Remove Row class
issue_body:
# Overview

After the introduction of keyed rows return we don't need to have `Row` class anymore. It was the extended tuple with headers access. For now just `tuple` will be enough again.

issue_title: Rebase compat on six
issue_body:
# Overview

`six` is a standard py2-py3 compat library and used in all other FD python packages. There is almost no difference what to use (the main use case to check something is a string) but custom `compat` need to be maintained, testes etc while `six` developers have done it for us.

issue_title: Builder incorrectly sources proxy env vars
issue_body:
Deis Builder appears to incorrectly source proxy environment variables when it starts up.

At [deis/builder/rootfs/bin/entry](https://github.com/deis/deis/blob/master/builder/rootfs/bin/entry) on line 5, the environment_proxy file is sourced:

```
if [[ -f /etc/environment_proxy ]]; then
	source /etc/environment_proxy
fi
```

The environment_proxy file is formatted for systemd EnvironmentFile consumption and does not `export` any variables.

When the docker daemon inside is started it fails to pull down the remote images for it to operate.

```
Sep 16 04:06:29 ip-10-116-11-55.platform.dev.atl-inf.io sh[2957]: [info] Downloading Docker images. This may take a long time. https://xkcd.com/303/
Sep 16 04:07:39 ip-10-116-11-55.platform.dev.atl-inf.io sh[2957]: [info] Docker: Sending build context to Docker daemon 17.41 kB
Sep 16 04:07:39 ip-10-116-11-55.platform.dev.atl-inf.io sh[2957]: Sending build context to Docker daemon
Sep 16 04:07:39 ip-10-116-11-55.platform.dev.atl-inf.io sh[2957]: Step 0 : FROM heroku/cedar:14
Sep 16 04:07:39 ip-10-116-11-55.platform.dev.atl-inf.io sh[2957]: Pulling repository heroku/cedar
Sep 16 04:07:39 ip-10-116-11-55.platform.dev.atl-inf.io sh[2957]: time="2015-09-16T04:07:39Z" level="info" msg="Get https://index.docker.io/v1/repositories/heroku/cedar/images: dial tcp 54.174.187.194:443: connection timed out"
Sep 16 04:07:39 ip-10-116-11-55.platform.dev.atl-inf.io sh[2957]: [error] Failed to build docker image: exit status 1
```

If I manually invoke the container, specifying `-e HTTP_PROXY=proxyhere` the container comes up and successfully pulls down the heroku/cedar image.

issue_title: Typo / errors in doc
issue_body: The api doc for globus_sdk.ClientCredentialsAuthorizer has a typo: "globus_sdk.ConfidentiallAppAuthClient" with two 'l' characters in Confidential, and includes a non-existent `scopes` parameter (which I think should be for `globus_sdk.ClientCredentialsAuthorizer`).

issue_title: Improve `requests` error wrapping to provide a more controlled surface for our package
issue_body:
We've seen `requests.exceptions.ConnectionError` come back to use with a body of `('Connection aborted.', BadStatusLine("''",))`. We then present that as a `GlobusConnectionError` to better encapsulate our usage of `requests` as an HTTP layer.

This is a confusing error from `httplib` being wrapped by `requests` being wrapped by us. It likely relates to a malformed response. The discussion on a [related, underlying issue](kennethreitz/requests#2364) in `requests` itself suggested the possibility of a proxy chopping off parts of encrypted data, and breaking decryption.

To make matters worse, on newer Pythons, `httplib` will potentially change, changing `requests` and therefore us. This is bad: people may try to rely on our error messages, and we should have more control.

So, let's should do something like the following:
- Let `GlobusNetworkError`s take a custom message and a contained exception
- Keep the underlying exception distinct -- don't pass its args back to our own exception class
  - Do we need to pass some of its args through?
  - Preserve the original traceback?

We may want to move the `RequestsException` handler out of our underlying caller and into a helper, just to avoid bulking up `BaseClient` too much.

issue_title: Trinity handling of KeyboardInterrupt
issue_body:
### What is wrong?

Currently, trinity is not correctly handling `KeyboardInterrupt` for asyncio loop based processes.  
### How can it be fixed

Fill this in if you know how to fix it.

We need to make use of the [`asyncio.loop.add_signal_handler`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/asyncio-eventloop.html#asyncio.AbstractEventLoop.add_signal_handler) API to do proper keyboard interrupt signal handling.

issue_title: Remove requirement files abstraction
issue_body:
By passing the `--with-requirements` flag, we try to retrieve requirement files (namely, `dev-requirements.txt` and `requirements.txt`) automatically from a pip installable archive or a local directory. While this might be nice for the user, it's not explicit and might confuse users. It is also not consistent what `pip` and `wheel` which require explicitly passing the relevant requirement files via multiple `-r` flags.

I suggest we remove that and allow users to pass multiple `-r` flags with explicit paths (local or URLs) to requirement files.

issue_title: itsdb.get_data_specifier() doesn't work with a unicode argument in Python2
issue_body:
This is a standard Python 2 unicode-vs-string issue. It prevents the main script from working with Python 2 for the `convert` command because (a) `from __future__ import unicode_literals` is used in `main.py`, and (b) the `convert` command has a default selector defined. To reproduce:

    $ python2 -m delphin.main convert <<< "[ ]"

Rather than ensure that the `convert` command passes a string,  a more robust fix would let `itsdb.get_data_specifier()` to work with unicode or (byte) string objects.

issue_title: tornado should depend on certifi
issue_body: The tornado package on PyPI depends on [certifi](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/certifi). The tornado conda package probably should, too. certifi is required to make HTTPS requests.

issue_title: Version pinning misses capability for Regular Expressions
issue_body:
I would like to restrict a package's version to release versions through pinning, excluding pre-release builds.
I think the only way to achieve this is using a regex for the version specification in the `pinned` file. But since there is no way to explicitly force a line in the pinned file to be marked a regex, [we can only get a regex-matching `VersionSpec` by satisfying this line in it's constructor](https://github.com/conda/conda/blob/231e9db898b3d7720b49e9e2050a88be6978fd38/conda/version.py#L319):
```
    elif '*' in spec.rstrip('*'):
```
So we would have to put multiple stars in the version spec but make sure that the resulting regex is still what we want to match.. plus, there's all kind of implicit replacementsdone to our regex, so this is not feasible I think.

Consider this example:
```
((maintenance_1.0.x)) wintermute:~$ conda search basemap
Fetching package metadata ...........
basemap                      1.0.6                np17py27_0  defaults        
                          .  1.0.7               np110py27_0  defaults        
                             1.0.8.dev           np110py27_0  conda-forge/linux-64 
                          *  1.0.8.dev0          np110py27_1  conda-forge/linux-64 
```
Let's assume I want to pin to any release version build of `1.0` series, excluding the "*.dev*" pre-release packages. I don't see a way to do that..

How about something like..
```
basemap ^1\.0\.[0-9]+$
```
and an if/else clause in the (admittedly already pretty cluttered) constructor of `VersionSpec`:
```python
elif spec.startswith("^") and spec.endswith("$"):
    self.spec = spec
    self.regex = re.compile(spec)
    self.match = self.regex_match_
```
That would enable people to use Regex in `pinned` file (detected by expicit start/end of string markers)..

issue_title: Conda update can result in binary incompatible packages when using multiple channels
issue_body:
Under instruction from @fbender in #1967, I'm upgrading this from a comment to its own issue. The TL;DR is that "conda update" can result in numpy from conda-forge with dependent packages from defaults, or vice versa, resulting in a binary incompatibility that only shows up at runtime.

Longer version:

Like many conda users, I more or less interchangeably install from defaults and conda-forge as the mood strikes me. My default channel from condarc, confusingly, is conda-forge, but some environments are based on defaults (for the MKL mostly). Now, conda-update in these environments does weird things. **My conda version was 4.3.11**.

Here I am trying to update all, specifying defaults as the channel. As you can see, it seems to work, except NumPy gets updated to conda-forge, thereby breaking everything. I type `y` while making a mental note to reinstall defaults numpy.

```console
 $ conda update --all -c defaults
Fetching package metadata ...........
Solving package specifications: .

Package plan for installation in environment /Users/nuneziglesiasj/anaconda/envs/36:

The following packages will be UPDATED:

    hdf5:    1.8.17-1                  --> 1.8.17-2                                 
    ipython: 5.3.0-py36_0  conda-forge --> 6.1.0-py36_0                             
    numpy:   1.12.1-py36_0             --> 1.12.1-py36_blas_openblas_200 conda-forge [blas_openblas]
    pillow:  4.1.1-py36_0              --> 4.2.0-py36_0                             
    s3fs:    0.1.0-py36_0              --> 0.1.1-py36_0                             

Proceed ([y]/n)? 

hdf5-1.8.17-2. 100% |############################################################| Time: 0:00:00  40.60 MB/s
pillow-4.2.0-p 100% |############################################################| Time: 0:00:00  53.43 MB/s
ipython-6.1.0- 100% |############################################################| Time: 0:00:00  23.39 MB/s
s3fs-0.1.1-py3 100% |############################################################| Time: 0:00:00  22.87 MB/s
```

Now to re-update numpy:

```console
 $ conda update -c defaults numpy
Fetching package metadata ...........
Solving package specifications: .

Package plan for installation in environment /Users/nuneziglesiasj/anaconda/envs/36:

The following packages will be UPDATED:

    scikit-learn: 0.18.2-np112py36_0                        --> 0.18.2-np112py36_blas_openblas_200 conda-forge [blas_openblas]
    scipy:        0.19.1-np112py36_0                        --> 0.19.1-np112py36_blas_openblas_200 conda-forge [blas_openblas]

The following packages will be SUPERCEDED by a higher-priority channel:

    numpy:        1.12.1-py36_blas_openblas_200 conda-forge [blas_openblas] --> 1.12.1-py36_0                                 

Proceed ([y]/n)? n
```

Right... Ok scrap that, what happens if I try to specify all those packages manually?

```console
 $ conda update -c defaults numpy scipy scikit-learn scikit-image pandas
Fetching package metadata ...........
Solving package specifications: .

Package plan for installation in environment /Users/nuneziglesiasj/anaconda/envs/36:

The following packages will be SUPERCEDED by a higher-priority channel:

    numpy: 1.12.1-py36_blas_openblas_200 conda-forge [blas_openblas] --> 1.12.1-py36_0

Proceed ([y]/n)? y
```

Whew!

My `.condarc`:

```yaml
channels:
  - conda-forge
  - defaults
```

Needless to say, a crisis was averted only because I know just enough about these things to be suspicious. A user described by @janschulz [here](https://github.com/conda/conda/issues/1967#issuecomment-180327850), really not very far removed from myself, would have been totally screwed:

> someone who has not a lot of ideas about requirements and such stuff and wants to have certain python packages to "just work".

So it seems `conda update` is still not there with @seibert's [Three Laws](https://github.com/conda/conda/issues/1967#issuecomment-172917291)...

> 1. `conda update` will not make the environment inconsistent or, through inaction, allow an environment to become inconsistent.
> 2. `conda update` will install the packages explicitly requested by the user on the command line, except when it conflicts with the First Law.
> 3. `conda update` will preserve the package state of environment, except when it conflicts with the First or Second Law.

What's the recommended way to deal with this...?

issue_title: BUG: different numpy version than requested
issue_body:
When requesting numpy 1.11, numpy-base 1.14 is pulled in, and the imported numpy is then 1.14 ( the problem happens in our continuous integration  https://travis-ci.org/nilearn/nilearn/jobs/399527033 ).

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Install a clean miniconda environment: 
```
wget http://repo.continuum.io/miniconda/Miniconda-latest-Linux-x86_64.sh
bash Miniconda-latest-Linux-x86_64.sh
```

Request the following conda packages:
```
conda install numpy=1.11 scikit-learn=0.17 scipy=0.17
```
(the bug happens when the 3 packages are requested together)

We get the wrong numpy version:
```
python -c "import numpy; print(numpy.__version__)"
```
prints '1.14.3'

I suspect that this is happening because the dependencies cannot be satisfied, but in this case, the lack of clear error is a problem. Also, this seems to be a regression. It was not happening until recently.

issue_title: Exit status of 99 for "No indices to act on" is inconvenient, make optional
issue_body:
As a rule we run all production shell scripts with exit-on-error enabled (we use [stringent](https://github.com/pottmi/stringent.sh/blob/master/stringent.sh). This has saved us from significant problems on many occasions.

I've recently updated our curator script to use curator v3 and discovered that the script, which contains several curator commands, will sometimes abort part way though due to a curator command reporting "No indices matched provided args." and returning a non-zero status.

The natural quick-fix would be to add `|| true` to commands that might fail. But this isn't a _failure_ and that 'fix' would mask real failures that we would want to know about.

A more complete fix requires extra shell coding to check the exit status and ignore the specific value of 99. That seems like a lot of effort to workaround this problem.

Please add an option to return a 0 exit status in the "No indices to act on" case.

issue_title: [FEATURE] add `intersect` option to `period` filter
issue_body:
Add `intersect` value to `stats_result` parameter of the `period` filter
which will identify those indices where `min_value <= range_to AND max_value >=
range_from` (the `period` of the filter and of the index data intersect).

The goal is to be able to identify indices that intersect with a given period, not just those that end or begin there. This is particularly useful for rollover indices.

issue_body:
It looks like the debian packaged elasticsearch-curator 4.2.6 is either built without libyaml support or missing the python-yaml dependency. This results in curator throwing a cryptic error on an otherwise valid yaml action file:

```
curator --config /etc/curator/curator.yml /etc/curator/delete_errors.action.yml 
/etc/curator/delete_errors.action.yml: 1: [: /usr/bin/curator: unexpected operator
```

Installing elasticsearch-curator via pip after installing both build-essential and python-yaml resolves my issue.

issue_title: AWS ElasticSearch service
issue_body:
With the new AWS ElasticSearch service they have the HTTP requests authenticated via IAM. With a couple tweaks I got a requests sigv4 plugin to work for this: https://github.com/sam-washington/requests-aws4auth/pull/2

The resulting code is pretty succinct (see the PR for how I'm connecting to ES) so maybe this would be a good addition to the documentation? Is anyone working on anything related to this?

issue_title: Set the Content-Type header on requests
issue_body:
Maybe I am misunderstanding the code, but it seems as if the actual http request (either urllib3 or requests) do no set the Content-Type. I would expect that at the very least, if the JSONSerializer was used that the Content-Type header would be set to "application/json" and something like "text/plain" if the payload is not JSON.

If my understanding is incorrect, then my apologies and feel free to close this ticket. If this is a valid concern and you are looking for a PR then I would be able to supply something in the coming weeks. 

As a side note, it looks like you have added support for arbitrary headers in the master branch, I think this could probably be used to set the Content-Type, but it seems to me like something more fundamental that you would always want set.

issue_title: Tag releases in docker
issue_body:
Currently azure-cli is at version 2.0.28 according to the [releases page](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/cli/azure/release-notes-azure-cli?view=azure-cli-latest) 

The latest version tagged in the [azure-cli-python repository](https://hub.docker.com/r/azuresdk/azure-cli-python/tags/) is version `:2.0.25` whereas the `:latest` has version 2.0.28 (`az --version`)

In our use case we have an image that extends the `azure-cli-python` image with a few scripts used in our CI.
We do not want to depend on a moving target like `:latest`, which can lead to inconsistencies between builds of the image. 
Currently there is a bug with the `az webapp deployment source config-zip` function in version 2.0.25 that makes it not work at all (#5440). This was fixed in a later version between `2.0.26` and `2.0.28`, but there is no version number tagged version that we can depend on after `2.0.25`.

It would be really excellent if new version releases were also tagged in docker again :-)

issue_title: 'KeyVault Secret Set' command unable to set tag as null
issue_body:
**Describe the bug**
Issuing a command like "az keyvault secret set --tag '' -n <some secret> --vault-name <some vault> "
will not set the tag as null instead it will only set it as "file-encoding": "utf-8".

Keyvault and Cli 2.0 Version:
azure-cli (2.0.33)
keyvault (2.0.22)


Below are some examples during the testing that we had:

Command:az keyvault secret set -n <some secret> --vault-name <some vault> --value "new_secret_value" --tags ''
{
  "attributes": {
    "created": "2018-06-13T01:06:59+00:00",
    "enabled": true,
    "expires": null,
    "notBefore": null,
    "recoveryLevel": "Purgeable",
    "updated": "2018-06-13T01:06:59+00:00"
  },
  "contentType": null,
  "id": "https://keyvault.vault.azure.net/secrets/Fake-Secret-connection/",
  "kid": null,
  "managed": null,
  **"tags": {
    "file-encoding": "utf-8"
  },**
  "value": "new_secret_value"
}

Then tried setting the tags with set-attributes command:

Command : az keyvault secret set-attributes -n <some secret> --vault-name <some vault>  --tags""
{
  "attributes": {
   "created": "2018-06-13T01:07:16+00:00",
    "enabled": true,
    "expires": null,
    "notBefore": null,
    "recoveryLevel": "Purgeable",
    "updated": "2018-06-13T01:07:40+00:00"
  },
  "contentType": null,
  "id": "https://keyvault.vault.azure.net/secrets/Fake-Secret-connection/",",
  "kid": null,
  "managed": null,
  **"tags": {},**
  "value": null
}

But if we use the old version CLI 1.0 npm we can have the output that we want tags are being set to null if we will not indicate any tags and that's the way it should I believe.

Also found out the package that was causing the tags to populate in Azure CLI 2.0.

DIR: /usr/lib64/az/lib/python2.7/site-packages/azure/keyvault/model/
File: secret_bundle.py

Code:

    _validation = {
        'kid': {'readonly': True},
        'managed': {'readonly': True},
    }

    _attribute_map = {
        'value': {'key': 'value', 'type': 'str'},
        'id': {'key': 'id', 'type': 'str'},
        'content_type': {'key': 'contentType', 'type': 'str'},
        'attributes': {'key': 'attributes', 'type': 'SecretAttributes'},
        'tags': {'key': 'tags', 'type': '{str}'},
        'kid': {'key': 'kid', 'type': 'str'},
        'managed': {'key': 'managed', 'type': 'bool'},
    }

    def __init__(self, value=None, id=None, content_type=None, attributes=None, tags=None):
        self.value = value
        self.id = id
        self.content_type = content_type
        self.attributes = attributes
        **self.tags = tags**
        self.kid = None
        self.managed = None

When I tried setting the self.tags to None instead of tags, azure cli 2.0 keyvault secret set command is working as it should.

Not sure if what I have above is good but please help me find a solution on this. thanks!

issue_title: Unable to install az on Ubuntu 18.04 w apt
issue_body:
**Describe the bug**
Unable to install a Microsoft signing key for `az` which works on Ubuntu 18.04 bionic and consequentially unable to install `az` via `apt`.

**To Reproduce**
Follow instructions in the document: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/cli/azure/install-azure-cli-apt?view=azure-cli-latest 
```
sudo apt-key adv --keyserver packages.microsoft.com --recv-keys 52E16F86FEE04B979B07E28DB02C46DF417A0893
Executing: /tmp/apt-key-gpghome.8anL89cvRR/gpg.1.sh --keyserver packages.microsoft.com --recv-keys 52E16F86FEE04B979B07E28DB02C46DF417A0893
gpg: connecting dirmngr at '/tmp/apt-key-gpghome.8anL89cvRR/S.dirmngr' failed: IPC connect call failed
gpg: keyserver receive failed: No dirmngr
```
The new signing key installs properly (`curl -L https://packages.microsoft.com/keys/microsoft.asc | sudo apt-key add -`, but this key still doesn't allow me to download from the `apt` source.
```
sudo apt-key list
...
pub   rsa2048 2015-10-28 [SC]
      BC52 8686 B50D 79E3 39D3  721C EB3E 94AD BE12 29CF
uid           [ unknown] Microsoft (Release signing) <gpgsecurity@microsoft.com>
```
Then this is the error when doing the `sudo apt-get update`
```
Err:4 https://packages.microsoft.com/repos/azure-cli bionic InRelease
  The following signatures couldn't be verified because the public key is not available: NO_PUBKEY B02C46DF417A0893
Reading package lists... Done
W: GPG error: https://packages.microsoft.com/repos/azure-cli bionic InRelease: The following signatures couldn't be verified because the public key is not available: NO_PUBKEY B02C46DF417A0893
E: The repository 'https://packages.microsoft.com/repos/azure-cli bionic InRelease' is not signed.
N: Updating from such a repository can't be done securely, and is therefore disabled by default.
N: See apt-secure(8) manpage for repository creation and user configuration details.
```

**Expected behavior**
Either the old or new signing key allows me to `sudo apt-get update` from the `az` apt source.

**Environment summary**
Bash on Windows with 18.04 (bionic) LTS

**Additional context**
`lsb-release` and `dirmngr` are installed and the latest versions.

issue_title: access subapp routes via <app-name>:<route-name>
issue_body:
From aio-libs/aiohttp-jinja2#163

> I think sub app routes should be available via `subapp:view-name`, which would require a `route_name` argument to `add_subapp`.

This would roughly mirror django routing and would make stuff like the `url()` jinja function work with subapps.

It would also make all `url_for` calls refering to subapps more succinct.

I think @asvetlov has some thoughts on implementation.

issue_title: refactor npm run build:css
issue_body:
It is ridiculously slow, and the step it takes are also ridiculous as:

 - `npm run build:css`
   - call `python setup.py css`
      - that call jsdeps
          - that call `npm install` (to get bower)
          - that call `npm run`
             - and call `bower install`
    - then build the css by calling less. 

Like really ? Asking the **build** the css invoke 3 different installation programs in 2 different languages. The build itself take a fraction of a second, for 2 files.

issue_title: project path is wrong if building with gitlab ci runner
issue_body:
Hi,

the path used for searching the report data is incorrect if building the project with a gitlab ci runner.

gitlab-ci-multi-runner provides a absolute path for the project directory. It was introduced by [this commit ](https://gitlab.com/gitlab-org/gitlab-ci-multi-runner/commit/0e50a4334aa0bb162e3e0c845401a369ae9841b0) and included in v0.7.

So it would be nice if you could revert the change introduced in your commit [564bc23f1542dd...](https://github.com/codecov/codecov-python/commit/564bc23f1542dd9de36e11b5867401e4c7a03769)


Thanks and regards
Philip

issue_title: Create job factories that automatically compute requirements
issue_body:
Instead of 

```
from toil_lib.tools import foo_job
job.addChild( foo_job, disk=PromisedRequirement( lambda x: x.size * 3.17, other_job.rv() ) )
```

We should be able to

```
from toil_lib.tools import foo_job_factory
job.addChild( **foo_job_factory( other_job.rv() ) )
```

This way the knowledge of how foo_job's disk requirement relates to the size of its input file is encapsulated in a library function that is colocated with the job function.

Instead of creating a factory, we could also convert foo_job from a job function to a class-based job, i.e. a subclass of Job. Those can define their own requirements in the constructor, IIRC.

issue_title: Coordinates on grid are used inconsistently throughout the code
issue_body:
I am opening this issue as a general case of this earlier one: #191 that identifies a specific mix up in the height/width of the canvas. A related question was asked in the mailing list.

- I noticed that the coordinates for `row, height, y` and `col, width, x` are used in the order `(x, y)` and `(y, x)` indiscriminately throughout the code.
- The naming of these indices also vary.

My suggestion is to proceed in two steps:
- First, we need to rename the variables to _row and _col throughout (that may or may not break the examples, depending on how class attributes are accessed). This is a deep undertaking as, as far as I can tell, every single class is affected; I doubt we can do this one class at a time.
- Second, (that will break the examples, and should be in the milestone "The Rebirth"), is to rewrite the classes using consistent signatures for all methods. My suggestion here is to use a `class Position` to encapsulate the indices; maybe a named Tuple, or an extension of named Tuples.
(depending on the timeline of "The Rebirth", we could maybe skip the first step and concentrate our energies on the second step)


Here are two examples extracted from #191:
**From Grid() in mesa.space.py:**
```
for row in range(self.height):
    for col in range(self.width):
        yield self.grid[row][col], col, row  # agent, x, y
 ---------------->>     Y    X     X    Y
```

**From `test_grid.py` :**
```
for agent in self.agents:
    x, y = agent.pos
    assert self.grid[y][x] == agent          # X, Y  <==>  Y, X
```

issue_title: Remove the SOAP api code from the repo (pycontrol)
issue_body:
The current library has a pycontrol module in it that manages a connection to the SOAP interface of the BigIP.  After talking with @jgruber he sees no reason for us to keep it given the new object/manager direction for the library.  We should be able to accomplish everything we need to with the REST API.

We should remove this code and if we need a SOAP session manager we should create an f5-icontrol-soap library like the [f5-icontrol-rest](https://github.com/F5Networks/f5-icontrol-rest-python) library.

I did some looking around at the library and it doesn't appear that anything really uses this except for SSL certificates, but after discussion we can do this with the REST API and a `SSLManager` to be written in the future.

issue_title: Default proxy issues
Http response JSON gets truncated
issue_body:
Hi we are getting a strange behavior with ASP.net core 2. We have created a simple web API that returns a large amount of JSON data (3.7MB) read from a static JSON file asynchronously. We have found that when we apply load on this application hosted in Azure web apps (> 50 requests) some of the responses contains JSON that is truncated. i.e. we get a status of 200 however the JSON is malformed and truncated. Our original hypothesis we thought was due to the size of the data and and have implemented compression to dis/prove this, however this issue still persists. We have found that this issue does not occur when the size of the Json is less than 1.5 MB and can handle requests > 250.We have also found that when the server resources are increased, i.e. we scale up or out on Azure (from basic) it reduces the frequency it occurs but does not remove it altogether.

To add to the above, we have carried out a similar exercise using ASP.net MVC and we do not get this behavior. The MVC application was also hosted on the same web app.

issue_title: TypeError: 'numpy.int64' object is not iterable
issue_body:
I am playing with a script that runs the same dataset on 7 sklearn classifiers. Just bumped into sklearn-crfsuite and decided to include it into the train-test loop. However, I am getting numpy type error on the following line:

```
self.classifier.fit(feats_train, feats_train)
```
where `self.classifier` is sklearn classifier instance, ` CRF(algorithm='lbfgs', c1=0.1, c2=0.1,
                           max_iterations=100, all_possible_transitions=True)` 
`feats_train` -- pandas Dataframe 
`targets_train` -- pandas Series

Concretely:

`feats_train`
```
      feat1  feat2    feat3  
0      -1       -1        7   
1      11       -1      702  
2      -1        5        1    
3       1       -1       74    
4      11        2       -1       
(...)
```
`feats_train`
```
        target
0       0
1       1
2       0
3       1
(...)
```

All sklearn classifiers trained and tested without errors on the same data while sklearn-crfsuite complains about the input having the same API. Is it a bug or should I modify my input somehow?

issue_body:
#726, #752 and #754 are affected by annoying race condition that causes Travis build to fail randomly (see https://travis-ci.org/letsencrypt/letsencrypt/builds/77715204, https://travis-ci.org/letsencrypt/letsencrypt/builds/78978888, https://travis-ci.org/letsencrypt/letsencrypt/builds/78990354, resp.).

It seems that manual authenticator doesn't manage to bootstrap on time before we proceed to `simple_verify`.
