issue_title: renew: letsencrypt.client:Registering without email!
issue_body:
I succesfully set up a certificate for https://turbolab.it/ (CentOS 6.7 with Apache 2.2) like this:

````
git clone https://github.com/letsencrypt/letsencrypt /opt/letsencrypt
cd /opt/letsencrypt
./letsencrypt-auto certonly --webroot -w /full/site/path/ -d turbolab.it -d www.turbolab.it
````

I provided my email + agreed to TOS from the *graphic* (blue) UI.

**Now I'm trying to stage the cert renewal but I'm facing issue**. When I try to renew with `./letsencrypt-auto renew --dry-run` I get:

```
[root@skycracker letsencrypt]# ./letsencrypt-auto renew --dry-run
Checking for new version...
Creating virtual environment...
Installing Python packages...
Installation succeeded.
Requesting root privileges to run letsencrypt...
   /root/.local/share/letsencrypt/bin/letsencrypt renew --dry-run
/root/.local/share/letsencrypt/lib/python2.6/site-packages/cryptography/__init__.py:26: DeprecationWarning: Python 2.6 is no longer supported by the Python core team, please upgrade your Python. A future version of cryptography will drop support for Python 2.6
  DeprecationWarning
Processing /etc/letsencrypt/renewal/turbolab.it.conf
2016-04-02 13:49:45,115:WARNING:letsencrypt.client:Registering without email!
2016-04-02 13:49:45,910:WARNING:letsencrypt.cli:Attempting to renew cert from /etc/letsencrypt/renewal/turbolab.it.conf produced an unexpected error: Missing command line flag or config entry for this setting:
Please read the Terms of Service at https://letsencrypt.org/documents/LE-SA-v1.0.1-July-27-2015.pdf. You must agree in order to register with the ACME server at https://acme-staging.api.letsencrypt.org/directory

(You can set this with the --agree-tos flag). Skipping.
** DRY RUN: simulating 'letsencrypt renew' close to cert expiry
**          (The test certificates below have not been saved.)

All renewal attempts failed. The following certs could not be renewed:
  /etc/letsencrypt/live/turbolab.it/fullchain.pem (failure)
** DRY RUN: simulating 'letsencrypt renew' close to cert expiry
**          (The test certificates above have not been saved.)
1 renew failure(s), 0 parse failure(s)
```

The `DeprecationWarning` makes me very uncomfortable, but I understood shouldn't be an issue.

The failure is the real issue here. I understood that the `renew` parameter should fire a renew with the same options used at install-time, so why the failure?

Thanks!

Update: I can confirm that `/etc/letsencrypt/renewal/turbolab.it.conf` holds the correct setting `
email = info@ MY SITE`

issue_title: Unable to create  or figure out gRPC stream with Go.
Producer sometimes fails to deliver when a broker is shutdown.
issue_body:
Description
===========

The producer sometimes fails to deliver messages when a broker is shutdown.

1. Baseline test. Result: All messages were delivered.
2. One broker shutdown upfront. Result: All messages were delivered.
3. One broker shutdown during the delivery of the messages. Result: Two times all messages were delivered and two times some messages failed to be delivered. The error message was:
>message failed: KafkaError{code=_MSG_TIMED_OUT,val=-192,str="Local: Message timed out"}

and in one fail case:
>_MSG_TIMED_OUT: kafka3:9092/3: 3 request(s) timed out: disconnect (average rtt 73.366ms)

Could it be that librdkafka sometimes fails to switch to the new leader of a partition after a broker is shutdown?

**Test setup:** 4 servers; 1 producer, 3 * kafka broker

**Topic setup:** bin/kafka-topics.sh --create --zookeeper kafka1:2181 --replication-factor 3 --partitions 3 --topic test

**confluent-kafka-python:** commit 7ebc807827b83e1d94861e2a59b4fb9bb12c10f0 (Oct 19 2017)

**librdkafka:** commit b9dd3a6e0ae127b76081cfee726bfb961cbd2649 (Dec 4 2017)
Apache Kafka: 1.0.0

How to reproduce
================

**Test script:**
```
import sys, time
from confluent_kafka import Producer

topic = "test"

def error_cb(kafka_error):

    with open("/tmp/kafa_errors", "a") as w:
        print("ERR", kafka_error.name(), kafka_error.str(), file=sys.stderr)
        w.write("{}: {}\n".format(kafka_error.name(), kafka_error.str()))


count_errors, count_success = 0, 0

def delivery_cb(err, msg):

    global count_errors
    global count_success

    if err:
        print('message failed:', err, file=sys.stderr)
        with open("/tmp/kafa_failed", "a") as w:
            w.write(str(msg.value()) + "\n")
        count_errors += 1
    else:
        count_success += 1


conf = {
    'error_cb': error_cb,
    'on_delivery': delivery_cb,
    'bootstrap.servers': 'kafka1:9092,kafka2:9092,kafka3:9092',
    "queue.buffering.max.ms": 100,
    "retries": 10000000,
    "socket.timeout.ms": 500,
    "default.topic.config": {
        "acks": "all",
        "message.timeout.ms": 5000,
    }
}

p = Producer(**conf)

with open("/tmp/kafa_test", "w") as w:

    for i in range(1000000):
        msg = "X" * 1000
        w.write(msg + "\n")

        while 1:
            try:
                p.produce('test', msg)
                break
            except BufferError as e:
                print(e, file=sys.stderr)
                time.sleep(1)  # queue full, wait
            p.poll(0)

p.flush()

print("total errors:", count_errors)
print("total success:", count_success)
print("total:", count_errors + count_success)
```



Checklist
=========
Please provide the following information:

 - [x] confluent-kafka-python and librdkafka version (`confluent_kafka.version()` and `confluent_kafka.libversion()`):
 - [x] Apache Kafka broker version:
 - [x] Client configuration: `{...}`
 - [ ] Operating system:
 - [x] Provide client logs (with `'debug': '..'` as necessary)
 - [ ] Provide broker log excerpts
 - [ ] Critical issue

issue_title: tzfile does not support non-integer minute offsets
issue_body:
Discovered this bug in #565 in the test for [Monrovia](https://www.timeanddate.com/time/zone/liberia/monrovia). I'm guessing that the problem is that before Python 3.6-ish, `datetime` didn't support this at all:

```python
>>> from dateutil import tz
>>> from datetime import *
>>> tzi = tz.tzoffset(None, timedelta(hours=12, seconds=30))
>>> datetime(2000, 1, 1, tzinfo=tzi)
datetime.datetime(2000, 1, 1, 0, 0, tzinfo=tzoffset(None, 43230))
>>> datetime(2000, 1, 1, tzinfo=tzi).utcoffset()
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
ValueError: tzinfo.utcoffset() must return a whole number of minutes
```

This should definitely be fixed in all versions of Python that support non-integer offsets, and the main question is what to do in the *other* cases. The options we have are:

1. Continue the current behavior of silently truncating to the nearest minute
2. Truncate the offset and add a warning in the *constructor* when a timezone with a non-integer number of minutes is constructed on a platform that doesn't support it.
3. Truncate the offset and add a warning in *utcoffset* or something when a platform doesn't support it.
4. Don't truncate at all and let `.utcoffset()` throw errors on platforms that don't support the non-truncated version.

I suspect that this affects a vanishingly small number of people, so my preferred solution is the one that has minimal impact on those people who are using timezones with integer number of minute offsets and people using a recent version of Python. I imagine that rules out a check in `utcoffset`. The lowest-cost thing to do is the fourth one, but that's the least friendly way. I'm thinking 2 is the right way to go.

Ideally we'll merge #580 and use that framework to distinguish between the different approaches.

This issue is currently blocking the merge of #565.

issue_title: Invalid byxxx values allowed in rrule constructor
issue_body:
The current value checking for `byyearday`, `bymonthday`, `bymonth`, etc, only validate that the combinations of `byxxx` rules don't lead to an infinite loop, however, they allow arbitrary values, e.g.:

```python
from dateutil.rrule import YEARLY, DAILY, rrule

rr = rrule(freq=DAILY, bymonthday=32, count=1)
print(list(rr))     # []

rr = rrule(freq=YEARLY, byyearday=377, count=1)
print(list(rr))     # []

rr = rrule(freq=YEARLY, bymonth=13, count=1)
print(list(rr))     # []
```

Actually running each of these takes a while, because the iterator will generate all the possible values before `byxxx` constraints and reject them if invalid, even though we know ahead of time that these constraints are invalid.

This can be fixed one of two ways, depending on what the RFC has to say about it:

1. We raise a `ValueError` when there are out-of-range value components if the RFC restricts all components to be valid values.
2. If the RFC explicitly says that you must accept invalid `byxxx` values, we add in a `_generator_empty` attribute that is set in the constructor, and if it's `True` just return immediately in the iteration step.
3. If the RFC makes no comment either way, we choose one of the above two (I am leaning towards `ValueError` because it's almost certainly a mistake, but that has API consequences that "empty generator" doesn't).

issue_title: SalesforceAutoField does not inherit from fields.AutoField
issue_body:
I'm running into an error running tests on a postgres (non-Salesforce) database. The issue is that django doesn't respect `SalesforceAutoField.get_internal_type`, and generates errors like the following:
```
IntegrityError: null value in column "Id" violates not-null constraint
```

Django is not properly viewing the field as an auto-increment primary key. I think this is caused by a bug in Django [here](https://github.com/django/django/blob/01ab84c61330ffa5ac87c637249611c5e5343e57/django/db/models/base.py#L800), where it checks whether the field is an instance of `AutoField` instead of checking that `f.get_internal_type() == 'AutoField'`. The Django source code seems to be inconsistent on this point, sometimes checking `get_internal_type`, and sometimes checking whether the field is an instance of `AutoField`. I'm going to submit a patch with test cases to Django, but an easy fix in `django-salesforce` itself would be to have `SalesforceAutofield` inherit from `fields.AutoField`.

## How to reproduce
Set your settings file to use a postgres db:
```python
DATABASES = {
    'default': {
        'NAME': 'something',
        'ENGINE': 'django.db.backends.postgresql_psycopg2',
        'USER': 'django',
        'PASSWORD': 'secret',
    },
}
```
Define a simple model:
```python
from salesforce import models as sf_models
from django.db import models

class Foo(sf_models.Model):
    class Meta:
        managed=True
```

Run:
 ```
$ DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE=django_salesforce_example python manage.py syncdb
$ DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE=django_salesforce_example python manage.py shell
```
Then run in the python shell run
```python
>>> from django_salesforce_example.models import *
>>> Foo.objects.create()
```
This generates the following stack trace:

issue_title: Possible to use disableClusteringAtZoom?
issue_body:
#### Please add a code sample or a nbviewer link, copy-pastable if possible

```
import folium
from folium.plugins import MarkerCluster

fmap = folium.Map(location=[53,-5.],zoom_start=7)
mc = MarkerCluster()
mc.add_child(folium.Marker(location=[53.34, -6.26],popup='one'))
mc.add_child(folium.Marker(location=[53.35, -6.26],popup='two'))

fmap.add_child(mc)
```
#### Problem description

Sometimes I have a lot of markers close to each other but I have to zoom in very far to be able to click on them. Leaflet has an argument disableClusteringAtZoom so in the html I can add:
```
disableClusteringAtZoom: 11
```
to `L.markerClusterGroup({})` and then they separate sooner.

Is it possible to add this to folium so I don't have to edit the html after or is there a way to already do this?

issue_title: Broken passing of context inside fields.List(fields.Nested(SomeSchema))
issue_body:
If schema is declared like this:
```python
class ChildSchema(Schema):
    foo = fields.Method('dump_foo')
    def dump_foo(self, obj):
        print 'context is', self.context
  
class ParentSchema(Schema):
    child = fields.List(fields.Nested(ChildSchema))
```
Then `context` is saved on first call and ignored on further calls:
```python
>>> ParentSchema(context={'bar': 1}).dump({'child': [{}]})
context is {'bar': 1}
MarshalResult(...)
>>> ParentSchema(context={'baz': 2}).dump({'child': [{}]})
context is {'bar': 1}
MarshalResult(...)
>>> ParentSchema().dump({'child': [{}]})
context is {'bar': 1}
MarshalResult(...)
```

It is because `copy.deepcopy(self._declared_fields)` doesn't copy `List.container` field and schema with `context` is cached inside `Nested` field and saved inside `_declared_fields`.

issue_title: Rename class Meta to class Options
issue_body:
Like [Schematics](https://schematics.readthedocs.io/en/latest/usage/models.html).

```python
class Meta
```

class Options is more semantically accurate for what this object is, and there's precedent for using that name, so I support renaming it.  For backward compatibility, marshmallow could check for the presence of either.

issue_title: create ModelChain(s) that implement very simple models
issue_body:
It could be useful to create a ModelChain or two that implements some kind of extremely basic system modeling. Something like ``power = user_def_const * poa_global``. The PVUSA model could be useful too. You all probably have more experience with sort of thing than I do.

A couple of motivations: 
* The simple models would make it easier to quantify just how much value the more sophisticated models add in different situations.
* The simple models might be a more gentle introduction to the library.
* It's easier to fit simple models to data, or at least to get started fitting.

I'm not yet sure if/how PVSystem would need to be modified to support this kind of thing. Maybe some additional keyword arguments in its constructor. Maybe you can reuse the module and inverter parameters arguments.

issue_title: Bug in claripy for negative FPs
issue_body:
So I think claripy (and consequently angr) has some issues with negative floating point numbers. It seems to acknowledge them internally, but at some points it can drop the sign.

Below I have an example of this happening, but in some of my code it happens deep in an AST and causes the AST to be Unsatisfiable, when really it shouldn't be.

```python
import angr
import claripy
from IPython import embed

def test1():
    print "Test 1:"
    x = claripy.FPS('x', claripy.FSORT_FLOAT)
    y = claripy.FPV(-1.0, claripy.FSORT_FLOAT)
    s = claripy.Solver()
    s.add(x == y)
    print s.satisfiable() #Prints out True
    print s.max(x) #Prints out 1.0 (Positive)
    print s.min(x) #Prints out 1.0 (Positive)

def test2():
    print "Test 2:"
    x = claripy.FPS('x', claripy.FSORT_FLOAT)
    w = claripy.FPV(1.0, claripy.FSORT_FLOAT)
    z = claripy.FPV(0.0, claripy.FSORT_FLOAT)
    s = claripy.Solver()
    s.add(claripy.fpEQ(claripy.fpAdd(claripy.fp.RM_RNE, x, w), z))
    print s.satisfiable() #Prints out True
    print s.max(x) #Prints out 1.0 (Positive)
    print s.min(x) #Prints out 1.0 (Positive)

def test3():
    print "Test 3:"
    b = angr.Project("/bin/true", load_options=dict(auto_load_libs=False))
    state = b.factory.entry_state()
    x = claripy.FPS('x', claripy.FSORT_FLOAT)
    y = claripy.FPV(1.0, claripy.FSORT_FLOAT)
    z = claripy.FPV(0.0, claripy.FSORT_FLOAT)
    state.add_constraints(claripy.fpAdd(claripy.fp.RM_RNE, x, y) == z)
    print state.se.satisfiable() #Prints out True
    print state.se.any_int(x) #Prints out 1.0 (Positive)


def main():
    test1()
    test2()
    test3()

if __name__=="__main__":
        main()
```

   File "/home/hep/arichard/git/ganga/python/Ganga/Lib/Splitters/GenericSplitter.py", line 117, in split                                                                                                                                      
    setattr(obj, attr, vallist[i])                                                                                                                                                                                                            
   File "/home/hep/arichard/git/ganga/python/Ganga/GPIDev/Base/Proxy.py", line 1030, in _setattr                                                                                                                                              
    GPIProxyObject.__setattr__(self, x, new_v)                                                                                                                                                                                                
   File "/home/hep/arichard/git/ganga/python/Ganga/GPIDev/Base/Proxy.py", line 651, in __set__                                                                                                                                                
    final_val = ProxyDataDescriptor._process_set_value(raw_obj, _val, attr_name)                                                                                                                                                              
   File "/home/hep/arichard/git/ganga/python/Ganga/GPIDev/Base/Proxy.py", line 569, in _process_set_value                                                                                                                                     
    val = ProxyDataDescriptor.__recursive_strip(_val)                                                                                                                                                                                         
   File "/home/hep/arichard/git/ganga/python/Ganga/GPIDev/Base/Proxy.py", line 549, in __recursive_strip                                                                                                                                      
    val.append(elem)                                                                                                                                                                                                                          
 AttributeError: 'unicode' object has no attribute 'append'  
```

This I believe is coming from the fact that in [Proxy.py:__recursive_strip#L531](https://github.com/ganga-devs/ganga/blob/develop/python/Ganga/GPIDev/Base/Proxy.py#L531) we explicitly check that the value is not a string i.e. type `str`. This doesn't catch the case where the value is a unicode string i.e. `u'HelloWorld'` which is of type `unicode`. To make sure that we catch both I recommend changing this to check for type `basestring` which will catch both as below:

```python
>>> issubclass(unicode, str)
False
>>> issubclass(unicode, basestring)
True
>>> issubclass(str, basestring)
True
```
---
**caveat** I Don't know that this is causing the problem yet, it's just my best guess. I'll check if this small patch fixes things and submit a PR if so.

issue_title: Reading ganga version from .gangarc in 6.1.12
issue_body:
The version stored in 6.1.11 is stored in the x,y.z format rather than the x-y-z format.

Shall we fix the 6.1.12 to be able to read x.y.z as well as x-y-z even though we're storing the format of x-y-z for forward/backward compatibility.
or,
Are we content in asking users to fix their .gangarc files by hand?

Users moving forwards/backwards are already familiar with changing/fixing this but users who just naively update to 6.1.11 and don't pay attention to what version they're on will see 6.1.12 crash on launch.

This is a minor fix from the users perspective but are we wanting to fix this ourselves or have this as a user requirement?

(We could even use this to intentionally break backwards compatibility if we chose in the future i.e. with Ganga-v7 if we ever go down this route.)

issue_title: Parallel hbond calculation
issue_body:
When analyzing hydrogen bonds with pytraj and pt.pmap 

`data = pt.pmap(pt.search_hbonds, traj, n_cores=4)`

following error occurs:

```
File "/home/janek/amber16/lib/python2.7/site-packages/pytraj/parallel/multiprocessing_.py", line 241, in pmap return _pmap(func, traj, *args, **kwd)

File "/home/janek/amber16/lib/python2.7/site-packages/pytraj/parallel/multiprocessing_.py", line 236, in _pmap return concat_dict((x[1] for x in data))

File "/home/janek/amber16/lib/python2.7/site-packages/pytraj/tools.py", line 287, in concat_dict
    new_dict[k] = np.concatenate((new_dict[k], d[k]))
KeyError: u'ASP23_OD1-THR27_N-H'

```
The serial run on the same dataset passes without any problems. My guess is that pytraj assumes that certain hydrogen bond will occur in all chunks of the trajectory analyzed on the specific cores (function concat_dict() ) which is not the case for the short-lasting interactions like for example ASP23_OD1-THR27_N-H (0.04 occurence in whole trajectory). Any help will be much appreciated.

As part of my research, I am using the D415 Realsense camera to capture depth images of an object randomly placed on a table (I am using only depth images). I used image detection to find the exact pixel in the depth image representing the center of the object, and I would like to know how to transform from that pixel position (row, column) in the depth image to the real Cartesian position (x,y) in the world, where the real depth and z are known (the images are always being taken from a certain known position above the table).
I used this code to retrieve to 3D point with respect to 'the center of the physical imager' (as explained [here](https://github.com/IntelRealSense/librealsense/wiki/Projection-in-RealSense-SDK-2.0#point-coordinates), I tested it with ppx and ppy values):
>pipeline = rs.pipeline()  
config = rs.config()
config.disable_stream(rs.stream.color)
pipeline.start(config)
frames = pipeline.wait_for_frames()
depth_frame = frames.get_depth_frame()
depth_intrin = depth_frame.profile.as_video_stream_profile().intrinsics
depth_value = 0.5
depth_pixel = [depth_intrin.ppx, depth_intrin.ppy]
depth_point = rs.rs2_deproject_pixel_to_point(depth_intrin, depth_pixel, depth_value)
print depth_point
>
The result is [0.0, 0.0, 0.5] as it should be.

I know where I placed the camera above the table, assuming that the center point would be the center of the front side of the camera (where the glass is), and this assumption is wrong. 

So after this introduction, **my question is where exactly is the location of 'the center of the physical imager' with respect to the center of the front side of the camera?**
Or maybe I should do some alignment before using rs2_deproject_pixel_to_point?

Thank you for your time!

issue_title: cmake version mismatches
issue_body:
It seems the update to `librealsense2` was only partially done.  Right now, in a parent project you have to do something like

```cmake
find_package(realsense2)
if(realsense2_FOUND)
  #                                   v
  list(APPEND THE_LIBS     ${realsense2_LIBRARY})
  list(APPEND THE_INCLUDES ${realsense_INCLUDE_DIR})
  #                                   X
endif()
```

The version numbers also need updating.  The following diff would make sense

```diff
--- a/CMake/realsense2Config.cmake.in
+++ b/CMake/realsense2Config.cmake.in
@@ -1,10 +1,10 @@
 @PACKAGE_INIT@
 
-set(realsense_VERSION_MAJOR "@REALSENSE_VERSION_MAJOR@")
-set(realsense_VERSION_MINOR "@REALSENSE_VERSION_MINOR@")
-set(realsense_VERSION_PATCH "@REALSENSE_VERSION_PATCH@")
+set(realsense2_VERSION_MAJOR "@REALSENSE_VERSION_MAJOR@")
+set(realsense2_VERSION_MINOR "@REALSENSE_VERSION_MINOR@")
+set(realsense2_VERSION_PATCH "@REALSENSE_VERSION_PATCH@")
 
-set_and_check(realsense_INCLUDE_DIR "@PACKAGE_CMAKE_INSTALL_INCLUDEDIR@")
+set_and_check(realsense2_INCLUDE_DIR "@PACKAGE_CMAKE_INSTALL_INCLUDEDIR@")
 
 include("${CMAKE_CURRENT_LIST_DIR}/realsense2Targets.cmake")
 set(realsense2_LIBRARY realsense2::realsense2)
```

however I didn't submit a PR because this could easily break things for people.  Maybe it would be better to, since people are already using these, set both?  I don't think there's a way of deprecating something like this in CMake.

issue_title: Queries do not support multiple inheritance
issue_body:
*Use Case: splitting out query definition logic into smaller and more manageable files (e.g. in Django app directories)*

Something like this will not currently work:

```python
schema = graphene.Schema(name='Swiftwind Schema')


class Query1(ObjectType):
    all_accounts = relay.ConnectionField(accounts_nodes.Account)
    account = relay.NodeField(accounts_nodes.Account)


class Query2(ObjectType):
    all_transactions = relay.ConnectionField(accounts_nodes.Transaction)
    transaction = relay.NodeField(accounts_nodes.Transaction)


class Query3(ObjectType):
    all_users = relay.ConnectionField(accounts_nodes.User)
    resolve_all_users = ConnectionResolver(accounts_nodes.User)


class Query(Query1, Query2, Query3):
    pass


schema.query = Query
```

It dies with the following error:

```
  File "/Users/adam/Envs/swiftwind/src/graphql-core/graphql/core/type/schema.py", line 30, in __init__
    assert isinstance(query, GraphQLObjectType), 'Schema query must be Object Type but got: {}.'.format(query)
```

I have also tried:

```python
schema = graphene.Schema(name='Swiftwind Schema')


class Query1(object):
    all_accounts = relay.ConnectionField(accounts_nodes.Account)
    account = relay.NodeField(accounts_nodes.Account)


class Query2(object):
    all_transactions = relay.ConnectionField(accounts_nodes.Transaction)
    transaction = relay.NodeField(accounts_nodes.Transaction)


class Query3(object):
    all_users = relay.ConnectionField(accounts_nodes.User)
    resolve_all_users = ConnectionResolver(accounts_nodes.User)


class Query(Query1, Query2, Query3, ObjectType):
    pass


schema.query = Query
```

Which dies with the error:

```
  File "/Users/adam/Envs/swiftwind/src/graphql-core/graphql/core/type/definition.py", line 211, in define_field_map
    ).format(type)
AssertionError: Query fields must be a mapping (dict / OrderedDict) with field names as keys or a function which returns such a mapping.
```

Presumably because the fields never get initialised?

Anyway, if this is possible I'd love to know how.

issue_title: How to manage an infinite stream of values in a subscription?
issue_body:
* GraphQL AioWS version:
    * aiohttp-graphql==1.0.0
    * graphene==2.0.1
    * graphql-core==2.0
    * graphql-relay==0.4.5
    * graphql-server-core==1.0.dev20170322001
    * graphql-ws==0.2.0
    * websockets==4.0.1
    * aiohttp==2.3.6
    * aiohttp-graphql==1.0.0
* Python version: 3.6.3
* Operating System: Ubuntu

### Description
I'm have a subscription going, an `async def` method (like the example in the readme) but it uses `while True` instead of `range`, so it never ends, it is constantly sending (`yield`) new values. I can connect to it using Relay, and I can see the frames coming in DevTools, and I can see the frame telling the GraphQL server to stop on that subscription, and then the frames stop coming.

### The problem
After the `stop` frame I get errors like this in the console:
```
2017-12-27 12:01:43,393 [25768] ERROR    asyncio: Task was destroyed but it is pending!
task: <Task pending coro=<AiohttpSubscriptionServer.on_start() done, defined at /home/mandx/graphql-server/.env/lib/python3.6/site-packages/graphql_ws/aiohttp.py:80> wait_for=<Future pending cb=[<TaskWakeupMethWrapper object at 0x7fcb0dc4b918>()]>>
```
And sometime I have to wait a bit for the server to shut down (by hitting `Ctrl-C`), like if there are more "tasks" pending.

This is the subscription class I'm using:
```python
class DataPoint(graphene.ObjectType):
    time = graphene.Float(required=True)
    value = graphene.Float(required=True)


class Subscriptions(graphene.ObjectType):
    roast_graph = graphene.List(DataPoint, roaster=graphene.ID(required=True))

    async def resolve_roast_graph(self, info, roaster=None):
        time = 0
        logger.debug('>>>>>>>> Start graph')
        while True:
            yield [DataPoint(time=time, value=randint(100, 400))]
            await asyncio.sleep(1.)
            time += 1
        logger.debug('>>>>>>>> End graph')
```
I can see the `Start graph` line being printed, but I never see the `End graph` line.

I guess what we need is some way of "breaking" the `while True:` loop from outside, right?

issue_title: update='append' not work anymore
issue_body:
simple code to reproduce
```Python
import numpy as np
import visdom
vis = visdom.Visdom()
vis.line(np.arange(3),np.arange(3),win='test')
vis.line(np.arange(3),np.arange(3)+3,win='test',update='append')
```
It used to work, but I update visdom and it won't work any more.
I guess the problem comes from [here](https://github.com/facebookresearch/visdom/blob/master/py/server.py#L422)

If I change it to :
```Python
        name = args.get('name')
        append = args.get('append')
        if name is None and append is None:
            return p  # we only updated the opts or layout
``` 
It works again. But I don't know whether it was the right way.

Since I use it a lot in my [project](https://github.com/chenyuntc/simple-faster-rcnn-pytorch/blob/master/util/vis_tool.py#L209-L213) and many other, I really hope it could be fix eariler.  

Thanks for your great job to create such a lightweight and powerful visulization tool!

issue_title: Resizable SVG for matplotlib plots
issue_body:
A simple change [here](https://github.com/facebookresearch/visdom/blob/master/py/visdom/__init__.py#L608) can be made to allow resizable SVG (it will take the size of the pane). All we need to do is to remove width and height attributes, something like this:
```python
if resizable:
    import bs4
    soup = bs4.BeautifulSoup(svg, 'html5lib')
    soup.svg.attrs.pop('width')
    soup.svg.attrs.pop('height')
    svg = str(soup)
```

issue_title: How to understand `fasttext dump`?
issue_body:
How to understand the output in `input.txt` and `output.txt`? The header lines are self-explanatory.  But how to know what each rest row correponds to?

```
$ fasttext skipgram -minCount 1 -dim 5 -input /dev/stdin -output /tmp/model <<< 'they have a happy well behaved dog'
$ fasttext dump /tmp/model.bin input > input.txt
$ fasttext dump /tmp/model.bin output > output.txt
$ head -n 3 input.txt output.txt 
==> input.txt <==
2000008 5
-0.165987 0.156645 -0.124124 -0.0407966 0.097405
0.024156 0.123827 0.00468502 0.198034 0.186645

==> output.txt <==
8 5
-0.000225553 -0.00219077 0.00456214 0.00115895 0.00347056
-0.000546314 -0.000476842 0.000296869 7.39911e-05 0.000176826
```

Also, what does `</s>` mean?

```
$ fasttext dump /tmp/model.bin dict > dict.txt
$ cat dict.txt 
8

happy 1 word
well 1 word

dog 1 word
</s> 1 word

django.db.utils.OperationalError: more than one primary key
issue_body:
**Steps to reproduce:**

* Add [the example LdapGroup class](https://github.com/django-ldapdb/django-ldapdb/blob/master/README.rst#using-django-ldapdb) to existing Django site
* Run `makemigrations`
* Run `migrate`
* Run `runserver`
* Log in as admin, visit LdapGroup entity

**What should happen:**

* Migration script for LdapGroup class created
* Migration script completes successfully
* Login successful
* LdapGroup contains groups on LDAP server

**What happens instead:**

* Migration script for LdapGroup class created (as expected)
* Error when running `migrate` (see below)
* Cannot proceed to `runserver` or visit admin app

```

sqlite3.OperationalError: table "ldapregister_ldapgroup" has more than one primary key

_models.py_

```
class LdapGroup(ldapdb.models.Model):
    """

issue_title: Screen on GUI should indicate how to deselect options
issue_body: Specifically, the "Which names would you like to activate HTTPS for?" screen should have a note to the effect of "Use spacebar to select and deselect options".

issue_title: Deprecate /etc/letsencrypt/{keys,csr}
issue_body:
Although these were added for super-plausible reasons about going back and finding stuff that people might need, I'm not sure if I've ever come across any case where someone successfully used their contents correctly (with the possible exception of people who naively deleted all of `/etc/letsencrypt/archive`), and I've come across many people who were confused by their presence, some of whom even thought that the CSR was the cert and that they were supposed to point Apache to the most recent CSR in `/etc/letsencrypt/csr` and the most recent key in `/etc/letsencrypt/keys`.

I think it is possible that almost no users manage to use these backups correctly and that they ought to be deprecated and removed.

issue_title: Support --certbot-dir?
issue_body:
There are a bunch of cases where for various reasons people want to change Certbot's directories, including running as non-root, testing things, and even [performance through parallelism](https://github.com/certbot/certbot/pull/4449#issuecomment-299802507). The UI for that is quite awkward, with three flags (`--config-dir`, `--work-dir` and `--logs-dir`) that the user has to provide.

Perhaps we should support a `--certbot-dir` flag that sets `--config-dir`, but also sets `--work-dir` to something like `$CONFIG_DIR/state` and `--logs-dir` to `$CONFIG_DIR/logs`, so the user only needs to set one flag.

issue_title: prevent modifications to certbot-auto and letsencrypt-auto
issue_body:
`certbot-auto` and `letsencrypt-auto` in the root of our repo should only be modified during our releases, however, this is not enforced by any of our tests. It should be enforced by something running in Travis.

Probably the easiest way to do that is to use `letsencrypt-auto-source/pieces/fetch.py` to download the latest stable copy of the script and diff that against `certbot-auto` and `letsencrypt-auto` in the root. If they differ, the test should fail.

issue_title: "renew" command fails in normal use without useful error
issue_body:
Hi,

I created my certificate with something along these lines:

```bash
./certbot-auto certonly -n -d www.domain1.com -d www.domain2.com --webroot --webroot-path /home/donmccurdy/public --expand
```

Later, to renew my certificate manually, and tried this:

```bash
./certbot-auto renew
```

Output:

```
➜  ~ ./certbot-auto renew 
Requesting root privileges to run certbot...
  /home/donmccurdy/.local/share/letsencrypt/bin/letsencrypt renew
Saving debug log to /var/log/letsencrypt/letsencrypt.log
Currently, the renew verb is only capable of renewing all installed certificates that are due to
be renewed; individual domains cannot be specified with this action. If you would like to renew
specific certificates, use the certonly command. The renew verb may provide other options for
selecting certificates to renew in the future.
```

I couldn't parse that error, and the log wasn't any more specific, so for the last few months I've been manually creating a new certificate instead of renewing the existing one. I finally tracked the problem down, [thanks to this thread](https://community.letsencrypt.org/t/cert-renewal-error/11278). An entry had appeared in `cli.ini` (a file I never edited myself) hardcoding those domains, and the `renew` command apparently refers to that file.

Because domains were not specified in the command I issued, could this error message be more explicit about why it is failing? Or perhaps call some attention to the setting it inherits from `cli.ini`?

Cheers

issue_title: Remove block numbers from the smart contract events
issue_body:
## Problem Definition

[NettingChannel](https://github.com/raiden-network/raiden/blob/b70cd3d5074ed4605b3a643834b74dc5c5604ed7/raiden/smart_contracts/NettingChannelContract.sol#L9-L12) expose a few events with the block number, that value is duplicated since it's available through the [eth_getFilterChanges](https://github.com/ethereum/wiki/wiki/JSON-RPC#eth_getfilterchanges)

> blockNumber: QUANTITY - the block number where this log was in. null when its pending. null when its pending log.

## Solution

Remove the `block_number` fields from all smart contract event and use the result from the JSON RPC call.

## Tasklist

- [ ] remove the block_number
  - [ ] ChannelNewBalance
  - [ ] ChannelClosed
  - [ ] TransferUpdated
  - [ ] ChannelSettled
- [x] Expose the `blockNumber` from the log to the [filter changes](https://github.com/raiden-network/raiden/blob/b70cd3d5074ed4605b3a643834b74dc5c5604ed7/raiden/network/rpc/client.py#L332-L357)
- [ ] Fix the code that needs the block number.

issue_title: Use a proper gas value per transaction
issue_body:
### Problem

The contract proxies are using the [Morden gaslimit](https://github.com/raiden-network/raiden/blob/d79e8c32b7c26f4fd57ca3b6efa8686815b5c5a4/raiden/network/rpc/client.py#L34) for each transaction, this should be replaced by an [estimated gas](https://github.com/ethereum/wiki/wiki/JavaScript-API#web3ethestimategas).

### Why it happened

The current JSON-RPC implementation used by raiden is from `pyethapp.rpc_client`, this implementation uses [`eth_gaslimit`](https://github.com/ethereum/pyethapp/blob/develop/pyethapp/rpc_client.py#L174-L175) if the user does not specify the transaction gas, [`eth_gaslimit`](https://github.com/ethereum/pyethapp/blob/develop/pyethapp/jsonrpc.py#L1008-L1011) is a pyethapp extensin that returns the latest block gas_limit, this result in transactions failling because of improper gas setting.

As a workaround the Morden gaslimit was used.

### Solution

1. Fix the pyethapp.rpc_client to use the estimated gas.
2. Switch to another JSONRPC library, like [web3.py](http://web3py.readthedocs.io/)

issue_title: Setting up environments before loading subkernels.
issue_body:
This is an extension to #695 and is related to #688 

On a multi-user environment, there can be multiple versions of the same interpreter that are loaded on demand with commands such as `module load`. Although users can add these commands to shell configuration files such as `.bashrc`, these files might not be executed when the Jupyter server is started remotely from JupyterHub. Also, using `.bashrc` limits users to a specific version of interpreter.

It therefore makes sense to define customized language modules (e.g. `R3.3`, `R3.4`) and associate them with certain commands (e.g. `module load R-3.3` or `PATH='/path/to/R3.3:$PATH`) so that the correct interpreter could be located before the kernel is started.

Implementation wise, instead of the default

```
subkernel name: R
language module: sos_R.kernel.sos_R
interpreter: what ever R in the path
kernel: ir
```

we could add in user or site configuration files multiple subkernels like

```
subkernel name: R-3.3
language module: R (or sos_R.kernel.sos_R)
environ:  path and variables, or commands to execute
kernel: ir
```

issue_title: Mutually Exclusive Traits
issue_body:
#### The problem
Traits are an awesome feature, but sometimes I build a factory where there are several traits that should never be used with each other. While I understand that it is an up to the user thing to make sure that people don't do something dumb with their factories and traits and documentation should be present to prevent people from using them incorrectly, a functional solution would also be great.

#### Proposed solution
Allow setting traits to False inside of each other. I believe this currently kind of exists, but if you set 2 different traits to false inside of each other, the execution of the factory throws an error indicating that there is a loop in the trait calls. I simply suggest an update to the logic of that check that can detect when a trait is negating another one instead of activating it, and allow that through, overriding whatever was placed on the actual factory call.

#### Extra notes
The traits are executed in alphabetical order as normal, and the value of a trait passed in as a kwarg on the factory call is overriden by the value from the other trait, for example the following factory setting the type of user to 'A' every time with the call made below because the admin trait is evaluated first and prevents the evaluation of the user trait.

Sample factory
```
class UserFactory(ModelFactory)
    type = factory.LazyAttribute(lambda: choice('A', 'U'))

    class Params:
        admin = factory.Trait(
            type='A',
            user=False
        )
        user = factory.Trait(
            type='U',
            admin=False
        )
```
Sample call
```
user = UserFactory(user=True, admin=True)
user.type == 'A'
```

issue_title: refer to instruments from another instrument
issue_body:
When trying to write a virtual gates instrument I encountered the following issue. First I create two drivers:

``` python
server_name='test'
ivvi1 = qcodes.instrument_drivers.QuTech.IVVI.IVVI(name='ivvi1', server_name=server_name, adress='ASRL2')
ivvi2 = qcodes.instrument_drivers.QuTech.IVVI.IVVI(name='ivvi2', server_name=server_name, adress='ASRL3')
```

Then I create a virtual instrument mapping gates to the combination of the two:

``` python
gate_map = {
    'L': (0, 8),  'M': (0, 9),  'R': (1, 11), ...
}
gates = virtual_gates(name='gates', gate_map=gate_map, server_name=server_name, instruments=[ivvi1, ivvi2])
```

The `virtual_gates` object (a subclass of `qcodes.Instrument`) automatically creates parameters with the names from `gate_map` and creates `get` and `set` functions mapping to the `IVVI` drivers. This works for `server_name=None`, but fails otherwise.

The problem is that when `virtual_gates` is created on a server the argument `instruments=[ivvi1, ivvi2` is being pickled, which is now allowed.

How to solve this issue?

issue_title: Something weird in padding+shuffling
issue_body:
Hello,

While building RNN models, I ran into a totally unexpected issue. 
Given working LSTM (or GRU) code, I had correctly decreasing train loss but increasing val loss.

ALL THINGS BEING EQUAL, changing batch_size from 32 to 4 caused the val loss to move in the correct direction and actually train.  I'm re-running with batch_size = 1 to confirm, but am pretty certain.

This seems potentially related to this "closed" issue.
https://github.com/pytorch/text/issues/70

Is there anything that would cause val/dev to not be properly sorted in larger batches?  My hypothesis is that sentences would then be extremely padded.  e.g. if you put a sentence of length 100 and 5 together, the latter would be padded 95 times.  Another reason might exist due to weird shuffling.  

If this hypothesis is correct, is there a way to fix this (ensure val/dev are sorted the same logical way train is)?  Hopefully a simple flag in BucketIterator or something similar.  
Will answer follow-up ?s or provide code if needed.

Hello,

I am have following code in my project:

```python

issue_title: Do not upload the checkpoint to Pulumi when there are no changes
issue_body: When processing a same operation, it appears we still upload the checkpoint to Pulumi, even though there is no change.  I believe in this cases we should not be doing this.  For no-op updates on stacks with many resources, this adds a substantial amount of wall-clock time for the update, and there is no observable output from the CLI.

issue_title: Creating actor and calling actor method immediately leads to hanging.
issue_body:
The following script currently hangs.

```python
import ray

ray.init()

@ray.remote
class Foo(object):
    def __init__(self):
        pass

    def bar(self):
        pass

ray.get(Foo.remote().bar.remote())
```

The reason is the following. There are three tasks invoked on this actor, `__init__`, `__ray_terminate__`, and the `bar`. And they get invoked in that order. That is, the actor handle destructor runs before the call to `bar`, so the `bar` method gets invoked after the actor has already been killed.

This suggests that the method invocation should have a reference to the actor handle so that the actor handle is still in scope while the method is being invoked.

issue_title: Spurious warnings re module test (patch)
issue_body:
j...@pobox.com, 2010-01-10T00:26:29.000Z 
rdflib's setup.py uses setuptools.find_packages, which finds module test in 
addition to modules rdflib and rdflib_tools.  As a result, the egg info 
installed by setuptools lists module test -- e.g. on my Ubuntu karmic 
system:

$ cat /usr/lib/pymodules/python2.6/rdflib-2.4.0.egg-info/top_level.txt
test
rdflib
rdflib_tools

For some reason I haven't investigated, module test doesn't actually appear 
to get installed.  That's a good thing, because Python itself comes with a 
module named "test".

However, the presence of "test" in top_level.txt causes spurious warnings 
when working on any other project on the same system that both 1. imports 
pkg_resources (which may happen indirectly, for example by importing a 
setuptools "namespace package" such as zope.interface) and 2. has a module 
named "test" -- for example, a file named test.py that runs that project's 
tests.

The solution would be to stop using find_packages and just list all of the 
packages in rdflib explicitly (rdflib has exactly two packages, after all -
- hardly a great maintenance burden):

sed -i -e 's/find_packages()/["rdflib", "rdflib_tools"]/' setup.py


What steps will reproduce the problem?

$ cat <<"EOF" > fail.sh
sudo apt-get install python-rdflib
dir=$(mktemp -d)
echo "$dir"
cd "$dir"
touch test.py
python -c "import test; import rdflib"
EOF
$ sh fail.sh

What is the expected output? What do you see instead?

Expect: No warning

Got:
...
/usr/lib/python2.6/dist-packages/zope/__init__.py:19: UserWarning: Module 
test was already imported from test.py, but /usr/lib/pymodules/python2.6 is 
being added to sys.path
  import pkg_resources


What version of the product are you using? On what operating system?

python-rdflib 2.4.0-5ubuntu1 Ubuntu 9.10 (karmic)

issue_title: Immediate manager eats exceptions
issue_body:
I forgot to set the author of the message, and I was left wondering why my message was never delivered despite not getting any errors with the immediate manager. Turns out there was an IndexError at message.py:112:
```
 return self.sender or self.author[0]
```
This exception was however silently discarded, which obviously should never happen. Instead, a friendly exception reminding me that at least one author must be set should've been raised (and delivered to my application!).
